name: Elizabeth Nader
subject: The worst budget offenders
body:
Peter, Guys, we have a budget. And per usual, it isn’t pretty. Let’s start with the topline number. At this point, we’re just throwing around money like it isn’t even real.
| | [ ]()
---
Peter, Guys, we have a budget. And per usual, it isn’t pretty. Let’s start with the topline number. **A whopping $58.8 billion.** At this point, we’re just throwing around money like it isn’t even real. Except, it’s very real and it’s coming out of your pocket and mine. And they’re admitting that we’re spending more than we have. But that’s all the Lunatics on the Left know how to do. And the “fiscally responsible” right has become too spineless to do anything about it. Governor Murphy is in his last year of treating us like his personal blank check and he’s really enjoying himself. But even he was more restrained than the Lunatics in the legislature who added on _even more_ bringing us to this GIANT bloated number. Here are a few of the biggest offenders as far as I’m concerned. (If you don’t feel like raising your blood pressure, you might want to stop reading).
* **$100 million for “Green Energy” projects** ... What else can we say on this, but ENOUGH already!
* **An _additional_ $70+ million for the failed (more accurately, the repeatedly and constantly _failing_) NJ Transit**. Because what government agency hasn’t improved when given more money with no accountability?
* **Get ready to lose even more on your property sale... The “fee” on property sales has gone up to 2% for home sales between $2 and $2.5 million,** with an additional percent for every 500,000, capped at 3.5% (how generous of them...), cutting into the profits of owners! At this point, it feels like they’re just trying to penalize desperate New Jerseyans trying to move to freedom while they suck as much as they can from you on the way out.

Get ready to see that line of Uhauls get a little bit longer... We the People will have something to say about this in November. Unfortunately, Governor Murphy isn’t going to feel that accountability. I want to hear from you, Common Sense friends. What are your biggest budget offenders? Shoot me an email and let me know! Stay cool! For New Jersey, Elizabeth Nader
---
| [ ]()
---

Hello, and thank you for taking the time to read this email. This message is part of an ongoing conversation between us, and I want to take a moment to ensure you have full transparency about why you received this message, how your information is handled, and what to expect from future emails. Why You’re Receiving This Email You’re receiving this email because at some point, you opted in to receive updates, news, or information on a specific topic we’ve previously discussed or shared. Whether it was through a subscription, a form submission, or another form of communication, your information was shared willingly, and we respect your decision to connect with us. If you’re wondering about the nature of our correspondence, rest assured that we aim to keep all emails relevant, timely, and free from unnecessary clutter. This includes respecting your inbox and refraining from sending irrelevant messages. Your Privacy is Important We value your trust and take your privacy very seriously. The information you provide is securely stored and never shared, sold, or used outside the purpose for which you provided it. If you ever want to review how we handle your data, feel free to contact us directly. Transparency is our priority, and we’re happy to address any questions you might have. You are always in control of the information you share. If you feel that any part of your subscription or interaction needs clarification, let us know. We’re here to provide accurate answers and ensure your satisfaction. How to Unsubscribe or Manage Your Preferences We understand that everyone’s inbox is different. If you ever find our emails no longer relevant, there’s no hard feelings. You can easily manage your email preferences or unsubscribe using the link provided below. By clicking the unsubscribe link, you’ll be taken to a page where you can either adjust your communication preferences (such as receiving fewer emails or only on specific topics) or completely remove yourself from our list. The process is straightforward, and any changes will take effect promptly. We don’t use tricks or gimmicks to keep you subscribed. Our priority is to ensure that our emails add value to your day, and if they don’t, we respect your decision to part ways. Accessibility and Communication If you have any trouble accessing the unsubscribe page, managing preferences, or understanding why you’re receiving this email, you can reach out directly to our support team at info@commonsenseclub.org. We aim to provide a response within a reasonable time frame and address your concerns effectively. Our communication is designed to be as inclusive as possible, but we know there’s always room for improvement. If you have any feedback on how we can make our emails more accessible or relevant, please don’t hesitate to share. A Commitment to Non-Intrusive Emails We aim to create a non-intrusive communication experience. This means we won’t overwhelm your inbox with excessive messages, and we work hard to ensure our content remains clear and concise. The purpose of this email is to stay connected with you and provide updates or information that we believe is meaningful. If we ever fail to meet these standards, we encourage you to let us know. Feedback, whether positive or constructive, is always appreciated. Your thoughts help us understand how we can do better and improve our communication approach. While we cannot guarantee every suggestion will be implemented, we will take the time to carefully review and consider your input. Contact Information If you’d like to get in touch with us outside of managing your preferences, here’s how you can reach us: Email: info@commonsenseclub.org
Phone: (866) 916-3221 (please note this is a text-only line)
Mailing Address:
Common Sense Club
165 Route 35
Red Bank, New Jersey, 07701 We strive to ensure all communication channels are open and readily available to you. Whether it’s a question, comment, or concern, our team is ready to assist. Legal Information We comply with all applicable laws and regulations regarding email communication. This includes adhering to anti-spam laws and maintaining the highest standards for consent-based communication. Your trust matters, and we work diligently to ensure every email you receive meets these requirements. If you need further clarification on our compliance policies or legal obligations, please feel free to ask. Transparency and accountability are central to our communication strategy, and we’re happy to provide additional details if needed. A Final Note Emails are one of the many ways we stay connected, but we understand they aren’t perfect for everyone. If there’s another way you’d prefer to communicate or stay updated, let us know. Whether it’s through social media, a direct call, or another channel, we’re open to finding the most effective way to share information with you. We want to emphasize that our goal is never to disrupt or clutter your inbox. Every email sent is intended to provide value, and we genuinely appreciate your time and attention. Thank you for allowing us to stay in touch with you. This footer was designed to ensure transparency, provide essential information, and give you full control over your communication preferences. We hope it meets your expectations, but if there’s anything you’d like to see improved, we’re always here to listen.

_Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District. _
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110793299/555343760/-1590518309?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YmVhYWJjYy0xZTU4LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hqOo1dFX1-pY6KbWXAsiQqMXEd3_Zz-7IrbPs6vS7-Q=&emci=4c2232f2-8f57-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=6beaabcc-1e58-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=7878003)

As one of just four Commonwealths — and carrying the motto Sic Semper Tyrannis — we know that government should be rooted in the will of the people. This isn’t rhetoric, Derek — this is generational work, and it’s just as important today as it was 249 years ago.
[**By making a contribution to DPVA today, you won’t be just marking Independence Day. You’ll be advancing it. You are invited to join our one-click issue response poll.
For data integrity purposes, please do not complete this survey if your email address is not dpwillis67@gmail.com .
The One Big Beautiful Bill Act, Donald Trump’s signature second-term domestic legislative package, is winding its way through Congress and is set to pass the House of Representatives today.
This bill is widely seen as the most hated piece of legislation in decades, driving up the deficit, killing green energy, slashing benefits for everyone who depends on Medicaid, and giving huge tax breaks to billionaires. Recent polling from Quinnipiac found that only 29% of Americans support the bill becoming law.
Team: We're looking to hear from our supporters ahead of this bill's final vote. Will you take our one-question poll right away?
Do you approve of Trump's so-called One Big Beautiful Bill?

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2]

name: Mark Ronchetti
subject: Proud to be an American? Only 36% of Democrats Are...What?!
body:
Plus, The Truth Behind the Big Beautiful Bill | New Episode Out Now for dpwillis67@gmail.com

| | | | | |
---
[|
---](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=3297ee95dc&e=567664f14c)
| | | | | Happy Independence Day from all of us at the **No Doubt About It Podcast**! We’ve got a brand-new July 4th edition that dives deep into patriotism, politics, and some explosive insights that you won't hear anywhere else.
🎧 [Watch the full episode now on YouTube ](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=42651f7aed&e=567664f14c)
---
[|
---](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=c6817e8e6c&e=567664f14c)
| **🇺🇸 Who’s Proud to Be an American?**
The Numbers Might Shock You We kick things off with a revealing Gallup poll showing how Republicans and Democrats differ dramatically when it comes to national pride. One party stands proud no matter what — the other? Well, let’s just say their patriotism comes with a political asterisk. Mark and Krysty unpack the data and what it means for our country moving forward.
**🏛️ The Big Beautiful Bill: Why It's a Game-Changer**
Forget the critics. The Big Beautiful Budget Bill is more likely to pass than people think — and here’s why the timing couldn’t be more strategic. Mark explains how the Trump administration learned from 2017, and why this early push could make all the difference in setting the tone heading into the midterm elections.
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=41815eba10&e=567664f14c)
---
| **📊 How Trump Gets It Done in Congress**
There’s a reason Trump keeps winning the loyalty of lawmakers — the numbers don’t lie. Go against him, and you're done in the next primary. We explain how this power dynamic is reshaping legislation, and why some New Mexico politicians are completely missing the boat by always voting with their party instead of fighting for local wins.

---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=021475f409&e=567664f14c)
---
| **🎥 July 4th Movie Pick:**
Don’t Miss This Brad Pitt Film Need something great to watch during your long weekend? We wrap the episode with a movie recommendation featuring Brad Pitt that’s a perfect blend of action, drama, and edge of your seat excitement. A must-watch for your Independence Day lineup!
---
|
---
| **👍 Support the Show:**

If you love the podcast, here’s how you can help us grow:

✓ Like the [episode](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=bbe4cec915&e=567664f14c) on YouTube
✓ [Subscribe and click](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=cb687b9f37&e=567664f14c) the 🔔 so you never miss a show
✓ [Rate and review](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=66e8519766&e=567664f14c) on your favorite podcast platform

✓ Donate to help keep the podcast going at: 🌐 [NoDoubtAboutItPodcast.com](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=fbe847235a&e=567664f14c)
Thanks for spending part of your July 4th with us. We’re proud to be Americans — and proud to share these conversations with you.
With gratitude and fireworks,

**Mark & Krysty Ronchetti **

| | Patriot, I was on the ground in Los Angeles—glass shattering overhead, people struck by debris, American flags burning, and police under attack. I felt a sense of menace and lawlessness. I couldn’t contain my frustration. **You don’t see these scenes in other states.** This didn’t happen by accident, it was by design. This is the result of fifteen years of one-party Democratic rule, creating an upside-down morality where those who do the wrong thing are rewarded while people who do the right thing are punished. Governor Newsom, Mayor Bass, and the local officials in Los Angeles have failed us. They let rioters run rampant while law-abiding Californians suffer. But there is hope. Californians want violent criminals removed from their communities, and they don’t want a government that allows riots. If we are ever going to win back California, it’s now while this is fresh in everyone’s minds. I’m running for governor to restore law and order, back our police, and make California golden again. But I can’t do it alone. I’m all in. Are you? **Here is how _you_ can help right now:**

name: Christian Urrutia
subject: Does Congress really need another lawyer? (yes)
body:
I’m running for Congress to meet the moment and fight for our country—because I’ve never sat on the sidelines when justice, service, or human rights were on the line.
| | | | | [](https://hqigqzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001_3qtjfIhCiAmyHnYJlXRCJpUEZtNZc_fn9THaNKNT69_wXH-8DftZGqfPQ2BdZdAv-hdb5Jo73tZMde01QMkQ5aZk8vmKG-huuoJhDa-YT9rFVb_G58HeoxfrAa_eeYHGpj9sj16EHMCH554BylI5Vg_4VYGy5TcDxyxJeG_GO2Tkp0_u0iW-JtQ3yFFZpvuu5pdWm1KWKCSNX_mEqEV1pRD3pN3JrpFKm3XLEua1E0=&c=vOaipe7rnuJZRXrepQz6BI89MzuPzs_zNvQDt53bz-IR4ppvpuznqA==&ch=NWvrYsp5O1_QW0Z9r57HwZOsbI6dvstoSkcuJ8hUXkbf6ofNj5N6xw==)
---
| | | | | [](https://hqigqzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001_3qtjfIhCiAmyHnYJlXRCJpUEZtNZc_fn9THaNKNT69_wXH-8DftZGqfPQ2BdZdAv-hdb5Jo73tZMde01QMkQ5aZk8vmKG-huuoJhDa-YT9rFVb_G58HeoxfrAa_eeYHGpj9sj16EHMCH554BylI5Vg_4VYGy5TcDxyxJeG_GO2Tkp0_u0iW-JtQ3yFFZpvuu5pdWm1KWKCSNX_mEqEV1uAbN_LfpnE1UA1wcYkDRmg=&c=vOaipe7rnuJZRXrepQz6BI89MzuPzs_zNvQDt53bz-IR4ppvpuznqA==&ch=NWvrYsp5O1_QW0Z9r57HwZOsbI6dvstoSkcuJ8hUXkbf6ofNj5N6xw==)
---
| | | | Peter,
My name is Christian Urrutia - I just announced I'm running for Congress in New Hampshire's 1st Congressional District because we all deserve better. We need change.
I wasn't born into politics or power. I fought to earn my seat at the table. I'm a Latino, the son of immigrants. English is my second language. I went to public schools and took my first job bagging groceries for five bucks an hour at Market Basket, New England's quintessential grocery store.
I put myself through the University of Iowa, substitute teaching during my school breaks, then graduated from Michigan Law during the financial crisis--$200,000 in debt and determined to serve.
---
| | | | | [](https://hqigqzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001_3qtjfIhCiAmyHnYJlXRCJpUEZtNZc_fn9THaNKNT69_wXH-8DftZGqfPQ2BdZdAKZIsCasPvH_VDlGLSM90roNFEqajebpLq3cCs1u8FkdFYngx8XYNTSKcYLzuXn5NYKSKSfBw-57uhROpMzG0FsDkt3fExjtZzfRASIZBbQkWYncT_pfhRqDw9hNinrn2CJLTPoKNYf6qH8Pu7s2b6lFwE1IWViFe3cxzccRC5-Y=&c=vOaipe7rnuJZRXrepQz6BI89MzuPzs_zNvQDt53bz-IR4ppvpuznqA==&ch=NWvrYsp5O1_QW0Z9r57HwZOsbI6dvstoSkcuJ8hUXkbf6ofNj5N6xw==)
---
| | | | So I began a legal career defined by fighting for underdogs, taking on big fights, serving our country, and standing up to power.
I first went to work at my local prosecutor's office and then went on to practice international law. As a young attorney, I took on a long shot pro bono case--fighting for the widow and children of Victor Jara, the Chilean folk singer who was tortured and executed for daring to stand up against the Pinochet dictatorship.
The regime tried to silence Jara's voice. But I believe justice never forgets, decades later, we brought his killer to trial in a U.S. courtroom. It was a fight against impunity, against fear, against forgetting. And we won - helping to pave the way for his killer to be extradited back to Chile to face justice.
At 35, I walked away from a successful legal career and enlisted in the military because I felt a duty to serve. I went through bootcamp, earned my commission at officer school, and now serve as a judge advocate in the National Guard. I have devoted my service as a military lawyer to defending soldiers when they've got no one else in their corner.
In 2020, President Biden appointed me to serve at the Pentagon--as Special Counsel in the Office of the Secretary of Defense. I advised the Secretary and the team on some of the toughest national security challenges we faced. And every day, I pushed for a foreign policy rooted in values--not hubris.
---
| | | | | [](https://hqigqzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001_3qtjfIhCiAmyHnYJlXRCJpUEZtNZc_fn9THaNKNT69_wXH-8DftZGqfPQ2BdZdAdkAwyoeUORJmtStua9k9STZUNz2nWNkI8hqLaz6hagbAnO9i7-KNhAALbaT9aTKZDxo8o3PFI0crl-uqETBX_GmZN-7qBVEwaJk_HVKiqc9dVFBuKROri31x2c-fm2-pGW9Zm9MKC3NesUb2mUpuijYeIAUJwF7G&c=vOaipe7rnuJZRXrepQz6BI89MzuPzs_zNvQDt53bz-IR4ppvpuznqA==&ch=NWvrYsp5O1_QW0Z9r57HwZOsbI6dvstoSkcuJ8hUXkbf6ofNj5N6xw==)
---
| | | | Watch our campaign launch video.
---
| | | | So does Congress really need another lawyer in its ranks?
Yes, it does--the kind of lawyer who fights for human rights, justice, and the Constitution.
I'm running for Congress because I know what it means to take on big fights -- I've been doing it my whole life. We need bold, progressive leadership-- someone unafraid to take on the powerful and fight for working people.
With the popular incumbent who holds this seat vacating it to run for Senate, if Democrats want to hold this swing district, which we are barely clinging onto by 3 points, we need a fighter. An outsider. Nominating someone from the establishment that got our nation into this mess will cost us this seat.
---
| | | | [And that's exactly why I'm in this race. Join me and let's keep NH-01 blue. Can I count on you to make a donation of $50, $25, or even $5, to help us launch this campaign with some firepower and momentum?](https://hqigqzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001_3qtjfIhCiAmyHnYJlXRCJpUEZtNZc_fn9THaNKNT69_wXH-8DftZGqfPQ2BdZdA4P18TSNcfgWMrHCyDteMD3-N6fotFCWAoo_CCmtppHTdsAewiDg-r9dZVIVwUyh9Nk9BTBi8AUJhtNIt1GN3r85T9g_gA-_dI5URxPle9LFeH_VpTs6oVF3xoJpp7no-oPSDk8DUkOyc4roGoeIXhoNPvpPGoVkfvYdCzB5icRY=&c=vOaipe7rnuJZRXrepQz6BI89MzuPzs_zNvQDt53bz-IR4ppvpuznqA==&ch=NWvrYsp5O1_QW0Z9r57HwZOsbI6dvstoSkcuJ8hUXkbf6ofNj5N6xw==)
---

clean_body: I’m running for Congress to meet the moment and fight for our country—because I’ve never sat on the sidelines when justice, service, or human rights were on the line. | | | | | == | | | | | == | | | | Peter, My name is Christian Urrutia - I just announced I'm running for Congress in New Hampshire's 1st Congressional District because we all deserve better. We need change. I wasn't born into politics or power. I fought to earn my seat at the table. I'm a Latino, the son of immigrants. English is my second language. I went to public schools and took my first job bagging groceries for five bucks an hour at Market Basket, New England's quintessential grocery store. I put myself through the University of Iowa, substitute teaching during my school breaks, then graduated from Michigan Law during the financial crisis--$200,000 in debt and determined to serve. | | | | | == | | | | So I began a legal career defined by fighting for underdogs, taking on big fights, serving our country, and standing up to power. I first went to work at my local prosecutor's office and then went on to practice international law. As a young attorney, I took on a long shot pro bono case--fighting for the widow and children of Victor Jara, the Chilean folk singer who was tortured and executed for daring to stand up against the Pinochet dictatorship. The regime tried to silence Jara's voice. But I believe justice never forgets, decades later, we brought his killer to trial in a U.S. courtroom. It was a fight against impunity, against fear, against forgetting. And we won - helping to pave the way for his killer to be extradited back to Chile to face justice. At 35, I walked away from a successful legal career and enlisted in the military because I felt a duty to serve. I went through bootcamp, earned my commission at officer school, and now serve as a judge advocate in the National Guard. I have devoted my service as a military lawyer to defending soldiers when they've got no one else in their corner. In 2020, President Biden appointed me to serve at the Pentagon--as Special Counsel in the Office of the Secretary of Defense. I advised the Secretary and the team on some of the toughest national security challenges we faced. And every day, I pushed for a foreign policy rooted in values--not hubris. | | | | | == | | | | Watch our campaign launch video. | | | | So does Congress really need another lawyer in its ranks? Yes, it does--the kind of lawyer who fights for human rights, justice, and the Constitution. I'm running for Congress because I know what it means to take on big fights -- I've been doing it my whole life. We need bold, progressive leadership-- someone unafraid to take on the powerful and fight for working people. With the popular incumbent who holds this seat vacating it to run for Senate, if Democrats want to hold this swing district, which we are barely clinging onto by 3 points, we need a fighter. An outsider. Nominating someone from the establishment that got our nation into this mess will cost us this seat. | | | | == | | | | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | == | | == | | | | | == | | == | | | | | == | | == | | | | | == | | | | == | | | | Let's fight for a future that finally delivers for working people. Thank you in advance for your support - only together can we make things better. Christian Urrutia Democrat for NH-01 | | | | | == | | | | Christian Urrutia is a member of the Army National Guard. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Army or the Department of Defense. | | | | | | | | | | == | | | | Mail checks to: Urrutia for New Hampshire PO Box 4531 Manchester, NH 03103 | | | | | == | | | | Urrutia for NH | PO Box 4531 | Manchester, NH 03103 US | |

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: Stop Noncitizen Voting—Zoom meetings start July 8th
body:
Reserve your seat now and help launch Only Citizens Vote Month.
| | | |

|

---
| **** Only Citizens Vote Month is right around the corner, and our upcoming Zoom trainings will give you everything you need to make your activities a success. For instance, did you know loopholes in current law allow non-citizens to register to vote?
In July, [**we 'll host four trainings**](https://qcgp-cmpzourl.maillist-manage.com/click/1edb9794ac7dbc0d/1edb9794ac7d5ece) explaining exactly how you can take part in Only Citizens Vote Month this August--and why educating voters about election-integrity reforms is crucial to a free America. If the SAVE Act doesn't pass, those loopholes in our registration process will remain.
The first training is **Tuesday, July 8th, at 2:00 p.m. ET**. The rest of the training webinars will be held on July 17th at 6:00 pm ET, July 22nd at 6:00 pm ET, and July 31st at 2:00 pm ET. Use the link below to register and secure your spot today, then share this email with friends and family who also want secure elections. **__****__****__****__** __ **__** **** ************

In liberty,
Jenny Beth Martin **__****__****__****__** __ **__** **** ************

name: The Democrats
subject: Here's why your support is so important right now.
body:
---
Derek, We’re about to ask you to **[donate directly to the DNC today.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-july-2025?refcode=em_20250703_3869_fr&amount=3&refcode2=3869_22930333_d48O7W&akid=3869%2E22930333%2Ed48O7W)** If you give us a moment of your time, we’ll explain why your support is so important right now. Here’s the situation: Trump and Republicans in Congress are scrambling to pass a budget bill that will devastate working families and slash funding for Medicaid, rural hospitals, and food assistance for children, veterans, and seniors. Meanwhile, the Republican Party and Trump’s Department of Justice are trying to use the Supreme Court to rewrite longstanding campaign finance law to benefit themselves. **There has never been a more important time for Democrats to take action, and the DNC isn’t sitting back.** We just launched Organizing Summer — a nationwide organizing effort to activate volunteers, register voters, and build Democratic power ahead of the 2025 elections and the 2026 midterms. And we’ve teamed up with the DSCC and DCCC to stop Republicans from manipulating campaign finance laws in their favor. Earlier this week, the Supreme Court said our fight goes forward. But the truth is, all of this work takes a lot to keep running. When we hit our fundraising goals, we’re able to allocate resources effectively — ensuring these programs remain strong and successful and that we can continue the fight against Trump. **That’s why reaching our fundraising goals month after month is so important. So here comes our question for you, Derek: **[Will you be one of the Democrats from Cresco we need to chip in a $3 donation to the DNC today and help fund our work?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-july-2025?refcode=em_20250703_3869_fr&amount=3&refcode2=3869_22930333_d48O7W&akid=3869%2E22930333%2Ed48O7W)**

name: Tim Scott
subject: Will you do me a favor?
body:
Peter, here’s the deal: Radical Democrats, Hollywood elites, and liberal megadonors are pulling out all the stops to beat my friend. |
---
[](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=dbac2f19e950401f99babb0b65ec4690&_e=CvgUUI_vwEtKa5TyR5KsCGt3U14325kq5E1WpTCGp4w34l9bkvm4bMDQfGSD4gIHfR7RUFCQr2M9_IbVZHB0A-ZiJ5LHZk_Ue043N3gLwk7X7T0ElSyl6mPzTnBT4Kvu1pLGIqX6u0EcDl8RDzyhooxsdBnV-XCu1wKhXsEBxAMOsZglY9TsNjniOIIbtmOnCqhSwfNTWfF18WafVU4TcmO4qc-fq76SEoWWJr94iaiS2Q9AC-bP1PvJolaXMR7VURP__5GIwfV2OLWjK24USgxDbK76NBR9VH1BLPtoww-1_NEmlVw343KJYKGyyEFAgSSNMYcRAp2ZMDoyiDJY30GWPtApLEgSRIxVtTfPjr4Sg7LhfHHrj8bCHUfYlMdwXxceHCQOfTSF5Od_KcTWCw_acEgrysSI4LF7B8FJEP8O89YkBRsvISIjc_37bMtgmCp4rhnebEyQd6cjMCnhZSiTljiYfEE2V_jjQMPWaB-JcvBZjLxHpdJiNfRuyHcnxzh5KgWQf3s91-SAx8g6bYV97tLtPAnO2GvrGvnXyYU4b4X8ST6LZcGOtXfIlvFC5hHS_r0FkIPMfgc-gKIQ0pwWMKT25m7LkWG_a-nAYZo2GxgiqQwB84bGBVtY9QpwHJVRI8TXmCs14vmiT-dsIIBO0gToMC_v52iTCSihzPW34drfFupvprMkCPfE7xf7c6O8WHE2uZ6Z-aLV7Tso-H18-2flVximlvEbxCzaLMo%3D)

Peter,

Without any doubt, South Carolina and our nation are better off because of the leadership of my friend Lindsey Graham in the United States Senate.

clean_body: Peter, here’s the deal: Radical Democrats, Hollywood elites, and liberal megadonors are pulling out all the stops to beat my friend. | Peter, Without any doubt, South Carolina and our nation are better off because of the leadership of my friend Lindsey Graham in the United States Senate. From chairing the Senate Budget Committee to being a key ally of President Trump, Senator Graham knows how to get things done. His problem-solving skills, compassion, and signature sense of humor make him a standout leader who rises to every challenge. I've witnessed firsthand how effective Lindsey Graham is at advancing our conservative values and working for the people of South Carolina. Peter, that is why I'm proud to support Senator Graham's re-election, and I hope you will too. Here's the deal: Radical Democrats, Hollywood elites, and liberal megadonors are pulling out all the stops to beat Lindsey -- AGAIN. The Left's smear campaigns and misleading ads have already begun, targeting our values and South Carolina's voice in the Senate. Democrats are laser-focused on flipping Republican seats to seize control of the Senate, and they've set their sights on one man: our fellow South Carolinian, Lindsey Graham. We can't let them succeed. | I saw what happened to Lindsey in 2020 -- the Left is ready to do it again this time around. He already has one far-left liberal opponent who is on MSNBC pleading for liberal dollars... These out-of-state liberals will funnel millions of dollars into the Palmetto State in an attempt to buy this election, defeat Senator Graham, win a Democrat majority in the Senate. They want to halt the tremendous progress we've already made for the American people working with President Trump. Whether he's supporting President Trump's America First agenda, rebuilding our military, or confirming conservative judges to our courts, Senator Graham gets the job done -- and he needs our help to stay in the fight. I trust Lindsey and I can count on you, Peter! Thanks, Tim Scott United States Senator (R-SC) In the United States Senate, South Carolina's Lindsey Graham has earned a reputation as a conservative, a problem-solver, and a leader who gets things done. He is a great friend to our men and women serving in uniform, a respected voice on national security matters, a leading advocate for cutting the out-of-control spending in Washington, and a champion for confirming conservative judges. | We want to make sure you're a proud member of Team Graham, and we don't want to bother you. We'd hate to see you go, but if you'd like to unsubscribe, please . | Paid for by Graham Majority Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Team Graham Inc, Fund for America's Future, Security Is Strength PAC, and NRSC.

clean_body: | | | ** Republicans are about to make their Big Brutal Bill law, cutting healthcare and vital programs in our communities. With their Big Brutal Bill, Republicans in Washington are sending a message to local governments: you’re on your own. Devastating cuts to healthcare, education, safety net programs, and more will put incredible strain on our cities, counties, and towns - and vulnerable communities will pay the price. We didn’t get here by accident. Right-wing extremists have been investing in local elections for decades, creating the farm team for higher office. At Local Democracy Project, we’re fighting back. We’re electing mayors, school board members, and city council members who will defend their communities from MAGA cuts. Just $1,000 in a local race can go a long way. ****If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. Thanks for your support, Local Democracy Project supports down-ballot races and empowering communities across the country. We’re rebuilding democracy from the ground up. To donate by check, please mail to: Local Democracy Project PO Box 720800 Pinion Hills, CA 92372 Paid for by Local Democracy Project. Contributions or gifts to Local Democracy Project are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. | | Sent via . name: Keith
subject: Our movement is powerful thanks to you
disclaimer_text: paid for by the Keith Ellison for Attorney General committee, PO Box 17224, Minneapolis, MN 55417
body:
Peter,

Thanks to your support, we didn’t just reach our end-of-quarter fundraising goal—we exceeded it.

When we come together to accomplish something like this, it sends a powerful message about the strength of our movement. Our campaign isn’t powered by billionaires or special interests, so we rely on thousands of people like you—people who care deeply about making sure every Minnesotan can afford their life and live with dignity, safety, and respect.

There’s a lot on the line right now, Peter. But once again, you proved we can count on you to show up when it matters most.

I'm grateful for your support,

Keith Ellison

Minnesota's Political Contribution Refund (PCR) program reset on January 1. You can now use your 2025 PCR to contribute up to $75 (or $150 for couples) and receive a full refund from the State of Minnesota.

Please take advantage of your PCR and make a REFUNDABLE $75 DONATION so Keith can continue fighting to help people afford their lives and live with safety, dignity, and respect.

CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250703ke?refcode=body&amount=25]

Prepared and paid for by the Keith Ellison for Attorney General committee, PO Box 17224, Minneapolis, MN 55417

Minneapolis, MN 55417
United States

Every single Democrat in the House is standing in opposition to this horrible bill that will create the single largest transfer of wealth from working people to the 1% in American history.
We need just FOUR Republicans to grow a spine, put people over politics, and kill this bill that slashes food assistance and Medicaid for millions of Americans. Rural hospitals in Arizona are at risk of closure if this bill passes. It’s a travesty that Republicans are more scared of Donald Trump’s wrath than of the harm this will bring to their own constituents.
[**That’s why I am asking right now****Peter,****: Will you call your member of Congress and demand that they kill this bill?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110795920/555351444/-1398297598?refcode=AU_YA_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20250703_AM1_V2_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MDY2MmU0Ni0yMzU4LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Do-V0RA2gmjYU5NoWtqC-KLRtV8zXSYbovBxj4tlMvE=&emci=10a0585f-2158-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=40662e46-2358-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=45157234)

clean_body: | | Every single Democrat in the House is standing in opposition to this horrible bill that will create the single largest transfer of wealth from working people to the 1% in American history. We need just FOUR Republicans to grow a spine, put people over politics, and kill this bill that slashes food assistance and Medicaid for millions of Americans. Rural hospitals in Arizona are at risk of closure if this bill passes. body:
I wanted to take a moment to send you this quick thank you note

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110796242/555352454/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZmM4M2JkNS0yMzU4LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GmKAPHax0t9hdFrE-u_GsuL0vBH8geRnbeZHN3wL6Mw=) I wanted to take a moment to send you this quick thank you note. Over the last week, I know my team and I have reached out... a lot. But thanks to you, we reached our End-of-Quarter fundraising goal with just a few minutes to spare, putting this campaign in a strong position for victory next year. But in times like these, it feels like as soon as one challenge ends, another begins. And we’re staring down the challenge of a lifetime right now. In the coming hours, the House is expected to vote on Donald Trump’s so-called “Big Beautiful Bill” —**the final vote before it is either sent to Trump’s desk, or defeated once and for all.** Last week, Mitch McConnell told us to “get over it” and just accept this Medicaid-slashing, cost-hiking scheme with no resistance. **But I refuse to give him that satisfaction.** I’m going to keep fighting against this bill with everything I’ve got, and I hope you will too. I know it’s easy to feel overwhelmed or powerless right now, but we can’t give up. name: Andy Beshear
subject: We've reached our goal, but there's more to do.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by In This Together PAC
body:
Continuing this work is going to take ALL of us, Peter. _I wanted to take a moment to personally thank you, Peter._

With your help, we hit our June fundraising goal. That’s no small thing — and it means we’ll be able to continue supporting candidates and leaders who will push back against extremism and return common sense to elected offices around the country.

But here’s the truth: This fight isn’t over. Not even close. Americans are ready for leaders who care more about helping real people than making headlines. With you by my side, we will put an end to the chaos and cruelty coming out of Washington by electing good people who will work to improve people’s lives every day in red and purple districts across the country.

With gratitude,

Andy

**P.S.** As we turn the page and look ahead to July, I’m hoping we can get a head start. [**If you’re able, chip in today so we can keep building momentum in the weeks ahead. >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110796703/555353526/841405539?refcode=em25_250702_eoqty_fnd_gq_act___AB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250702_eoqty_fnd_gq_act___AB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=4ff424d0-2458-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzNjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNGZmNDI0ZDAtMjQ1OC1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Jd76FptunoPLEMR451Gase2ccK_Z0_MYtILWet9xa8o=)

name: New PA Project Education Fund
subject: Happy Fourth of July!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund
body:
<a clicktracking=off href="http://example.com">
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/NPP_Ed_logo_RGB_green1-01.jpg]
Hi Friend,
Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter!
Earlier this week, the PA [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] House State Government Committee [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house.
An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills.
HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.”
State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [https://www.politicspa.com/state-government-committee-sends-voter-id-election-omnibus-bills-to-house-floor/141399] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.”
HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.”
Among other changes, [https://penncapital-star.com/campaigns-elections/voter-id-and-vote-by-mail-reform-bills-move-to-pa-house] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers.
The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed.
The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error.
Upcoming events
As Allegheny County voters prepare for the pivotal 2025 local, municipal and judicial elections, the New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is hosting two critical public candidate forums to give community members the chance to hear directly from the candidates shaping our schools and courts. These moderated discussions will allow voters to ask questions and learn more about each candidate’s priorities and qualifications before heading to the polls.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Pittsburgh%20School%20board%20forum%208.5x11.png]
Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools and students.
Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader.
With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color.
When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221
RSVP [https://click.everyaction.com/k/106793662/542679746/-435660973]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Allegheny%20judicial%20flyer.png]
Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot.
Your vote shapes our justice system!
Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager
Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms.

[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/7237344C447445787878394747342B75584E47375A57552F745265427662334B544A4A36505A58657A5871474B7A5A6E53705571424758325657365969365747/editor_images/WCM%20logo.JPG] #CourtsMatter
This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [https://www.democracydocket.com/news-alerts/a-double-win-for-voters-in-pennsylvania] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls.
First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/445-2025-04-24-Order-denying-PA-Attorney-Generals-motion-to-intervene.pdf] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots.
The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/cases/pennsylvania-undated-and-wrongly-dated-mail-in-ballots-challenge-eakin/] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted.
After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/441-2025-04-02-Notice-Of-Appeal-.pdf] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary.
Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as Citizen_AG, filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/1-2024-10-29-Complaint.pdf] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act.
The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls.
District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/67-2025-05-02-Memorandum-.pdf] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place.
In law, standing [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standing_(law)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case.
In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money]
The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests.
In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money] .
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/DelCo_2.png] We are hiring!
Let's provide civic education together!
We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania.
Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote!
Positions start at $20 an hour.
Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org]
EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org]
News You Can Use
There are things every one of us can do [https://www.democracydocket.com/opinion/we-have-more-power-than-trump-wants-us-to-believe-heres-what-you-can-do/] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last.
1. Educate Yourself
Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally.
Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more.
* New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pittsburgh-mayor-election-2025-candidates-quiz]
* Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections/assistant/]
* Everything you need to know about mail voting [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-mail-ballot-how-to-request-fill-out-return] , becoming a poll worker [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide]
* Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pennsylvania-primary-election-day-2025-voter-information-registration]
* Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-commonwealth-superior-court-candidates] , vetting judicial candidates [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-candidate-vetting-guide]
* Guides to State College school board [https://www.spotlightpa.org/statecollege/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-state-college-school-board-anderson-huff-barlow-black-desmarais-mcternan-sogor] , Reading City Council president [https://www.spotlightpa.org/berks/2025/04/reading-city-council-president-primary-election-2025]
* Guides to voting as a college student [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-college-student-voting-guide]
En Español
* Guía para votar por correo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pensilvania-elecciones-primarias-2025-votar-correo-como-llenar] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/reading-concejo-municipal-primarias-elecciones-2025]
* Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-primarias-elecciones-2025-votar-informacion] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-elecciones-2025-como-convertirse-trabajador-electoral]
2. Stay Engaged
To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win.
Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [https://www.inquirer.com/politics/pennsylvania/montgomery-county-commissioner-neil-makhija-emmy-20250508.html] )
3. Share What You Learn
Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting.
Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year!
4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations
Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work.
Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above!
5. Become a Trained Poll Worker
Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process.
Here are some resources that may be helpful:
Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [https://penncapital-star.com/briefs/advocacy-groups-offer-training-for-pa-primary-poll-watchers/]
Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide/]
Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [https://fcfreepresspa.com/poll-workers-needed-for-pennsylvanias-2025-primary-election/]
6. Engage Your Elected Officials
Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise.
7. Vote in Every Election
Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility.
Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th!

clean_body: [] Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter! Earlier this week, the PA [] House State Government Committee [] [] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house. An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills. HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.” State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.” HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.” Among other changes, [] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers. The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed. The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error. District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place. In law, standing [)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case. In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [] The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests. In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [] . [] We are hiring! Let's provide civic education together! We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania. Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote! Positions start at $20 an hour. Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org] EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org] News You Can Use There are things every one of us can do [] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last. 1. Educate Yourself Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally. Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more. New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [] Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [] Everything you need to know about mail voting [] , becoming a poll worker [] Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [] Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [] , vetting judicial candidates [] Guides to State College school board [] , Reading City Council president [] Guides to voting as a college student [] En Español Guía para votar por correo [] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [] * Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [] 2. Stay Engaged To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win. Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [] ) 3. Share What You Learn Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting. Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year! 4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work. Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above! 5. Become a Trained Poll Worker Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process. Here are some resources that may be helpful: Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [] Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [] Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [] 6. Engage Your Elected Officials Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise. 7. Vote in Every Election Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility. Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th! Recent Events [] Solidarity for Immigrants On Monday, May 5th, also Cinco de Mayo, over 300 people, representing 18 community organizations from across Pennsylvania, gathered at the State Capitol in Harrisburg to rally for immigrant rights. We amplified voices that have been silenced, advocated against injustice, and showed that solidarity is not just a word—it is a force that creates change. This event was a declaration that Pennsylvania stands against injustice and division. [] [] [] NPPEF on the Ground [] Last Monday, May 5th was the last day to register to vote for the primary election! Our teams were out in their communities leading up to the voter registration deadline on Monday. We helped folks ensure they have the ability to exercise their right to vote in the primary on Tuesday, May 20th. Our teams are currently getting ready for GOTV - a time of year where we are on the ground connecting with our communities and mobilizing them to vote. It's our favorite time of year to build relationships with those we hope to uplift. Dauphin County [] [] Delaware County [] [] Luzerne County [] [] Super voters vote twice a year, every year ! Thank you for doing your part to stay informed and engaged! [] Last day to register to request a mail ballot: Tuesday, May 13th Primary Election: Tuesday, May 20th Last day to register to vote for the General Election: Monday, October 20th Last day to request mail-in ballot: Tuesday, October 28th General Election: Tuesday, November 4th Important Links: Voter Hotlines: PA Department of State Website [vote.pa.gov] Voter Protection Hotline: 1-866-OUR-VOTE Upcoming elections [] DOS PA Voter Hotline: 1-877-VOTESPA Mail-in and Absentee Ballot [] Spanish/English Hotline: 1-866-VE-Y-VOTA Fact-Checking Election Claims [] Asian Languages Hotline: 1-888-API-VOTE Arabic/English Hotline: 1-844-YALLA-US Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund New PA Project Education Fund PO Box 453 West Chester, PA 19381 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

body:
News, events, and action items to keep Nebraska red and growing strong.
| | | | |
---
| Letter from the Chair
---

Happy Independence Day!
July 4th has been celebrated in many ways over my lifetime. I've celebrated with fireworks and cookouts to in-home celebrations with friends reciting the Declaration of Independence and sharing stories of our country's founding. A tradition since moving to Nebraska has been Douglas County Republican Party's annual Pancake Breakfast at the Elmwood Park Pavilion. Candidates and officeholders serve patrons as the Pancake Man flips row after row of battered goodness.
Whatever you do and wherever you are, stop to appreciate the providential founding of our country's history. We are all part of "the great experiment" that produced a great nation formed by a people filled with an independent spirit and an "anything is possible" work ethic.
My hope this Independence Day is that we have a revival of the spirit of the founding fathers as President Trump paves the way for an economic expansion through his efforts at world peace, increased production of energy, permanent tax cuts, influx of manufacturing and technology investment, and reduction of government regulation.
Americans are being invited to continue the journey started by Washington, Adams, and Jefferson. Let's pause to remember but then prepare ourselves for the work ahead to rebuild our economy, strengthen our resolve, and revive our spirit.
Mary Jane Truemper Chairman, Nebraska Republican Party
---
---
| |
---
---
| |
# What's Happening...
---
Candidate Recruitment
With the 2026 primary filing deadline less than a year away, the Nebraska Republican Party (NEGOP) is actively recruiting candidates for over 450 offices, including 101 currently held by Democrats that we aim to flip. A full list of these offices, districts, and current officeholders is attached.
To lead this effort, we're launching a Candidate Recruitment Committee to help identify and support strong Republican candidates across the state. If you or someone you know is interested in running, reach out to political@ne.gop -- I'd love to connect.
Not sure which district you're in? Use the Nebraska Voter Information Lookup Tool to find your precinct, polling location, and races tied to your address. Sample ballots will be available closer to the election.
To empower our candidates, the NEGOP is hosting its second [Campaign and Candidate School](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001HJOfaC-KGAgErtTogN8xOjWWxjCAj1cgdtOILjz2JuJhnF9pW9F0L7VIFbA4u2AklyRluiTDw2QzmhH6NTBGE2x70DnkfEhXvC9RmGEHbv7olK_-xw6kNnAZvRQ9TGrPWLdLm1EM140rpvgaVf7yQHHfBPjWLR07sbGMVuxhaf1rqW9WcWWa0OLrrYcmtX4D&c=AheEg0AtJeM5s6naIEMSLvE5sbLxxsnthNZs_nn60DA1d7bIZ36Svg==&ch=FqxhU0U0U0AulH6_cJZ4aXnec0sYWy201dJFVTq31FcQFWcm28LVJw==) of the 2026 cycle on July 26, 2025, in Lincoln, Nebraska, from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Partnering with the Republican National Committee (RNC), this training will equip candidates, campaign staff, volunteers, and grassroots leaders with the tools, strategies, and knowledge to run winning campaigns. Topics include:

* Ready to Run: Your Path to Public Office
* Campaign Organization: Building a Winning Team
* Path to Victory: Strategic Campaign Planning
* Campaign Budget: Financial Planning and Management
* Field Program: Effective Voter Contact Strategies
* Absentee & Early Voting: Maximizing AB/EV Opportunities
* Mastering Campaign Finance: Funding Strategies and Best Practices
* Fundraising Methods: Building a Strong Financial Base
* Data-Driven Victory: Leveraging RNC Tools, GOP Data Center, and Numinar
* Crafting Your Narrative: Messaging, Rapid Response, Earned and Paid Media, and Building a Communications Plan

Register now for the Campaign and Candidate School: [https://secure.anedot.com/negop/campaignschool2025](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001HJOfaC-KGAgErtTogN8xOjWWxjCAj1cgdtOILjz2JuJhnF9pW9F0L7VIFbA4u2AklyRluiTDw2QzmhH6NTBGE2x70DnkfEhXvC9RmGEHbv7olK_-xw6kNnAZvRQ9TGrPWLdLm1EM140rpvgaVf7yQHHfBPjWLR07sbGMVuxhaf1rqW9WcWWa0K7x8epoR1FH&c=AheEg0AtJeM5s6naIEMSLvE5sbLxxsnthNZs_nn60DA1d7bIZ36Svg==&ch=FqxhU0U0U0AulH6_cJZ4aXnec0sYWy201dJFVTq31FcQFWcm28LVJw==) Together, we can build a stronger Nebraska. Join us in this critical effort to shape our state's future!
Best, Joe Hagerty Political Director political@ne.gop

name: Cori Bush
subject: Betrayal.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POLITIVIST ACTION PAC
body:
https://www.politivistaction.com [https://www.politivistaction.com]
Hi Peter,
The Senate GOP just passed a devastating budget, and the House is not too far behind. Let's call a spade a spade—this bill is a betrayal of the majority of our nation’s families.
Trump’s budget will kick approximately 17 million Americans off their health care. It includes the largest cuts to Medicaid in history. And experts warn that 50,000 people will die unnecessarily every year because of it.
It’s outrageous. It’s immoral. And it’s a direct attack on our families, our health, and our future.
When I was in Congress, I often went toe-to-toe with my GOP counterparts—unafraid to call out their disastrous policy positions for what they were: attacks on our communities, our safety and stability, and our rights.
Peter, we need more bold, fearless, Democratic leaders who won’t back down. Leaders who will fight budgets like this and help us build a country where everyone has access to care, housing, and justice. Because this budget should never have seen the light of day.
Your donation today will help us support progressive champions who are ready to confront this betrayal and fight for the people. Can I count on you to support and elect our next generation of progressive champions? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q3?refcode=x_250703_gopbudget_act]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

clean_body: [] Hi Peter, The Senate GOP just passed a devastating budget, and the House is not too far behind. Let's call a spade a spade—this bill is a betrayal of the majority of our nation’s families. Trump’s budget will kick approximately 17 million Americans off their health care. It includes the largest cuts to Medicaid in history. And experts warn that 50,000 people will die unnecessarily every year because of it. It’s outrageous. It’s immoral. And it’s a direct attack on our families, our health, and our future. When I was in Congress, I often went toe-to-toe with my GOP counterparts—unafraid to call out their disastrous policy positions for what they were: attacks on our communities, our safety and stability, and our rights. Peter, we need more bold, fearless, Democratic leaders who won’t back down. Leaders who will fight budgets like this and help us build a country where everyone has access to care, housing, and justice. Because this budget should never have seen the light of day. Your donation today will help us support progressive champions who are ready to confront this betrayal and fight for the people. Can I count on you to support and elect our next generation of progressive champions? [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: DONATE $5 [] DONATE $10 [] DONATE $25 [] DONATE $50 [] DONATE $100 [] OTHER [] Trump’s budget is a declaration of war on vulnerable families. But with your support, we’ll fight back—and win. The Trump administration clearly believes it can act with impunity when it comes to the law – **and it should alarm every American.**

In just a few months, Trump has:
–**Targeted opponents** with politically motivated investigations
–**Frozen Congressionally-approved funding** over personal and political disagreements
–**Ordered military action without required congressional authorization**

Trump’s abuse of power to advance his personal and partisan agenda is putting American lives and our democracy at risk.

**New Dems believe no one — _not even a former president_ — is above the law.**

That’s why Democrats have introduced the ‘No Kings Act’ to restore checks and balances, prevent presidential abuse of power, and reaffirm that in America, we govern by the rule of law, not by personal whims!

name: Team Scalise
subject: Karoline Leavitt makes a DEFIANT STATEMENT all voters must read immediately:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
She struck gold with this one.

President Trump's FEARLESS Press Secretary - Karoline Leavitt - makes a DEFIANT STATEMENT all voters (who still support Trump) must read immediately:[](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zii86niroam15av9197ga7sg6f10b9kc6bsfei70_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cljm4o74c9h2bs)"The Democrats are doubling down on craziness and radical policies that are undermining the very fabric of our country. Just look at the Democrat nominee for NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani - he's a Democrat Socialist... Meanwhile, under President Trump's leadership, Republicans are fighting tirelessly for everyday, Middle Class Americans who reelected this President and who are carrying out a revolution of common sense"She struck gold with this one.
Zohran Mamdani is the canary in the coal mine for everything to come if Conservatives back down and allow the Democrat Machine to barge back into power.
Look, we've seen the Democrats' true face since President Trump took office. Since the SECOND he put his hand on the Bible, they threw decency and commonsense out the window to unleash a revolution of their own.
They're hell-bent on taking down Conservatives - and our country with them.

If we surrender power, they'll IMMEDIATELY move to impeach President Trump, shut down the MAGA agenda, ram through the woke agenda, and charge ahead to investigate our President and every single one of his allies.
We can't let that happen.
House Republicans are doing EVERYTHING they can to hold the line but it's time for Conservatives everywhere to help us out and take a stand.
So here's what we're asking:
We're pleading with EVERY SINGLE Conservative reading this email to go on the record right here and right now and confirm their support for the entire MAGA movement. [It's going to take a grassroots army - approximately 1,000,000 strong - to help us hold the line and take on the Democrat Machine.](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z97pfacd0qcqk2p82npqqo3dt44ca25mo0ff9qd0_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cljm4o74c9h2bs)
Can we rely on you to be one of them?[GO ON THE RECORD >>
](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zlp1d25ssbsen50iou1hk9ekomn0bpa1ocsgi89g_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cljm4o74c9h2bs)Friend- not only does the Democrat Machine have Congressional Democrats... But they have Hollywood bigwigs, scores of Silicon Valley elites, and powerful lobbyists on speed dial.
Unless we want the country we all know and love to become a socialist paradise, we need your help right here and right now.
[And STEP ONE is confirming whether or not you're on our side.](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zbe3u16i3jbvmve2pbik5c81o6bh7segh4ihhem0_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cljm4o74c9h2bs)
We can't hold the line alone.
- House Republicans(Sent via Team Scalise)[GO ON THE RECORD >>
](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z876je1ovjvggrriecrj84m1fh5sk4sdfp2m8m28_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cljm4o74c9h2bs)
* * *
\- Democrat Leadership formed an _anti-Trump task force_ \- More than a dozen Attorneys General are _suing_ the Trump Administration\- A House Democrat just introduced _7 Articles of Impeachment_[](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zaf6uka1e7omu1pdtr9afhckpgc1vtlf0tr2dh50_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cljm4o74c9h2bs) If they manage to strip us of our House Majority, they'll have the power to impeach, investigate, and bring his entire legislative agenda to a SCREECHING HALT!
**We're PLEADING WITH YOU to do your part to help SAVE our Pro-Trump House Majority:**[ DO YOUR PART: PITCH IN NOW
](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zg2qedqd8hbk0uqm8ebuiddeqn33s0v2vq1rir98_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cljm4o74c9h2bs)| | |
