name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: I voted HELL NO on the Big Bad Betrayal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
It’s a massive attack on poor and working people.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20250703-5209-footer&t=1&akid=5209%2E420207%2EXoKWWf)
Peter,
Today I voted **HELL NO** on Trump’s Big Bad Betrayal Bill and I will never give up the fight to stand up for every American, who all deserve so much better than this.
Everything in this bill is structured to lift up the wealthiest millionaires, billionaires and giant corporations. They get _gold bars_ while poor and working Americans get _breadcrumbs_.
**Here is who loses in this betrayal:**
❌ **17 million Americans** will have their health care ripped away and health care costs will increase for everyone.
❌ Over 300 rural hospitals and 500 nursing homes that will shutter, and the **Planned Parenthood clinics that are defunded.**
❌ Millions of hungry families lose their food assistance with the **largest SNAP program cut in history.**
**And here is who wins (spoiler alert, it’s not poor and working people):**
💰 The wealthiest billionaires and millionaires get **$5 trillion in tax breaks.**
🚨 ICE gets $45 billion more to supercharge their **kidnapping and disappearing of people** of all legal statuses.
We can’t afford to stop fighting.
**We _must_ keep organizing** and building our movement so that we can stop MAGA Republicans and build a government that puts working families first.
I’m working to do that every single day, but I can’t do it without you. I don’t take a dime of corporate PAC money, and I don’t have a rolodex full of billionaires that I can lean on for favors. This campaign is powered by the people, which is why I need your support today.
[**Peter, if you’re with me, will you pitch in $3 today to help grow our progressive movement and keep up the fight to achieve social, economic, and racial justice for _everyone?_**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250703-1?refcode=em20250703-5209&amount=3&refcode2=5209_420207_XoKWWf&akid=5209%2E420207%2EXoKWWf)
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250703-1?amount=3&refcode=em20250703-5209&refcode2=5209_420207_XoKWWf&akid=5209%2E420207%2EXoKWWf)**
In solidarity,
Pramila Jayapal
P.S. – Don’t forget to [**sign up for our next Resistance Lab training on July 13!**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/50195?t=2&akid=5209%2E420207%2EXoKWWf) This is our way of transforming fear and frustration into action. You’ll join the more than 10,000 folks from all 50 states who’ve joined one of our sessions since we started just three months ago!
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20250703-5209-footer&t=3&refcode2=5209_420207_XoKWWf&akid=5209%2E420207%2EXoKWWf)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=4&akid=5209%2E420207%2EXoKWWf)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=5209%2E420207%2EXoKWWf)

Paid for by Pramila for Congress

name: Accountability Action via Our Revolution
subject: Big Beautiful NIGHTMARE

body:
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32532?t=1011&refcode=email-20250703_v&akid=8964%2E5885482%2ET2EhlC ]Our Revolution logo

Peter Willis,

Congress just passed Donald Trump's disastrous "Big Beautiful Bill" – a
tax scam delivering a staggering windfall to billionaires and corporations
while leaving working families behind.

16 million will lose health care. Millions of families will go hungry –
all so billionaires can buy new yachts and private jets.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32532?source=email-20250703&t=1060&akid=8964%2E5885482%2ET2EhlC ]Bernie tweet decrying how unpatriotic this reconciliation bill is, a
distaster to throw 16 million Americans off of healthcare.

We're INCREDIBLY angry and we know you are too. But together we're going
to turn that anger into action.

Our Revolution is launching an unprecedented electoral campaign targeting
vulnerable lawmakers who voted "YES" to this monstrous billionaire
giveaway. We're scaling up NOW in key states to train volunteers, grow our
base, and activate the masses to make Trump and his puppets in Congress
pay for what they've done. Then we'll rally behind progressive
candidates to ensure we elect fighters up and down the ballot who will
stand with the working-class over billionaires.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32532?t=1057&amount=22&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&refcode=email-20250703nd&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=8964%2E5885482%2ET2EhlC ]This fight may be over. But the larger one is just beginning. Can you chip
in right now to help Our Revolution hold these politicians accountable in
battleground states across the country?

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation to Our Revolution will process immediately.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32532?t=1042&express_lane=true&amount=9&refcode=email-20250703nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=8964%2E5885482%2ET2EhlC ]CHIP IN $9 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32532?t=1043&express_lane=true&amount=22&refcode=email-20250703nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=8964%2E5885482%2ET2EhlC ]CHIP IN $22
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32532?t=1045&express_lane=true&amount=27&refcode=email-20250703nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=8964%2E5885482%2ET2EhlC ]CHIP IN $27 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32532?t=1046&express_lane=true&amount=46&refcode=email-20250703nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=8964%2E5885482%2ET2EhlC ]CHIP IN $46
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32532?t=1047&express_lane=true&amount=66&refcode=email-20250703nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=8964%2E5885482%2ET2EhlC ]CHIP IN $66 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32532?t=1048&amount=9&refcode=email-20250703nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=8964%2E5885482%2ET2EhlC ]CHIP IN OTHER AMOUNT

In the lead up to the vote, our team spent countless hours targeting
vulnerable House and Senate Republicans -- talking to our members in their
districts, delivering their stories to their offices, and mobilizing them
to reach out to their Reps. Over and over again we heard incredibly
powerful stories:

“I'm a disabled vet and I'm extremely concerned that all my benefits will
be cut and I'll be homeless. I'm subsiding on SSI and VA benefits solely
and it is entirely up to Congress's whim whether I can count on those
services. I have no confidence whatsoever that my military service is
valued.”

And while the vote may be over, the anger people across the country are
feeling is NOT. Which is why Our Revolution is just getting started
channeling that anger into political power – mobilizing grassroots
volunteers, training progressive candidates, and pushing bold, progressive
alternatives to rebuild our economy for working folks, not Wall Street
billionaires.

This fight isn't just about resisting Trump's agenda – it's about
fundamentally shifting political power back to the people. It's about
sending a clear message: representatives who enrich billionaires at our
expense will pay the price at the polls.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32532?t=1054&amount=22&refcode=email-20250703nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=8964%2E5885482%2ET2EhlC ]We're working around the clock to scale up in key states to deliver our
message loud and clear. Can you make a donation today to power Our
Revolution's efforts to defeat lawmakers who betrayed the working-class?

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

body:
Proud to be delivering WINS for all Americans.| | | Friend, When I campaigned for North Carolina’s Sixth Congressional District, **I promised to deliver on an American First agenda. **

I’m proud to say I’m **DELIVERING** by:

🇺🇲 Providing working families the largest middle-class tax cut in history
🇺🇲 Blocking $3.4 Trillion in wasteful spending over the next 10 years
🇺🇲 Giving ICE and Border Patrol the tools they need to go on offense
🇺🇲 Funding the Border Wall and President Trump’s deportation plan
🇺🇲 Giving the Coast Guard the ships and support they need to defend our shores from the flow of fentanyl

Our country will be safer and the bloated federal government will have less waste. There will be NO tax on tips, NO tax on overtime, and LOWER taxes for our seniors.

Proud to be delivering WINS for all Americans of all ages: for my parents, for my peers, and for my children. America First, always.

Thank you,

Extreme House Republicans just approved the largest cut to Medicaid and food assistance in American history to fund tax breaks for their billionaire donors.
[The latest GOP stunt will rip healthcare away from 17 MILLION Americans, jack up our utility bills and take food out of the mouths of children, seniors and veterans. It’s disgusting.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110816736/555419996/588388208?amount=10&refcode=20250703_em_fr_rr1_np4c_sur_main&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDIvMS84ODQ2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNjg3ZDI2MS00YTU4LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CVUv02XBi9EcpQLq-ima-fYYWek7V7vAIanlAHZlSB4=)
I won’t be silent. You shouldn’t be either.

name: Justice Democrats
subject: BREAKING: Trump’s “Big Ugly Bill” passes Congress
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

They did it, Peter.

Republicans barely but successfully passed their budget bill in both the
Senate and now the House, sending it to Donald Trump’s desk for signature.

This is the largest wealth transfer in history from poor and working-class
people to millionaires and billionaires.

It will throw more than 16 million people off their health insurance, cut
$1 trillion from Medicaid which will in turn shutter many nursing homes
and rural hospitals, take nutrition assistance away from hungry kids and
families who need it, eliminate hundreds of thousands of clean energy jobs
while jeopardizing billions of dollars in wind and solar energy
investments, cut Pell Grants for millions of students pursuing higher
education, and more.

All to reward the already ultra-wealthy and dump billions of dollars into
ICE’s secret police to further terrorize our communities.

According to Republicans, it’s okay to add $4 trillion to the national
debt as long as that money is being funneled to the wealthy and our
increasingly militarized police state — but forget about our tax dollars
being used to actually help the public!

To make matters worse, Republicans have strategically made it so many of
these cuts won’t take effect until AFTER the 2026 midterm elections.

That way, people won’t feel the impact until it’s too late to make their
voices heard at the ballot box, after their Republican members have
already won re-election.

This budget is a disgrace, and the Democratic Party needs to spend the
next year making sure that every single voter knows how their Republican
representatives threw them under the bus.

Justice Democrats is actively recruiting and supporting working-class,
progressive candidates to challenge BOTH Republican and Do-Nothing
Democratic incumbents, so we can take back Congress and start putting
working people first.

Today is a sad day for the American people, but our fight is far from
over.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250703-1?refcode=em20250703-36814&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=36814_1203867_1c7o3x&akid=36814%2E1203867%2E1c7o3x ]Contribute

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250703-36814-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36814_1203867_1c7o3x&akid=36814%2E1203867%2E1c7o3x

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36814%2E1203867%2E1c7o3x

name: Sarah McBride
subject: Make them pay for their cruelty
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
We need to make sure the GOP feels the consequences.
#
**House Republicans just passed Donald Trump’s Big Ugly Bill — and now it’s headed to his desk.**
This heartless legislation is so cruel and so callous, it’s hard to even type the words.
It will leave 17 million more Americans uninsured and force the rest of us to pay higher premiums and higher out-of-pocket medical expenses.
It will shutter rural hospitals and healthcare facilities.
It slashes SNAP benefits, ripping food off the table of families already struggling to get by.
It punishes the most vulnerable to reward billionaires.
**That’s why I voted no.**
But House Republicans passed this bill anyway, and the mass devastation it will cause will be felt by families everywhere.
**We need to make sure the GOP feels the consequences. That starts with taking back the House next year, and that work begins today.**
[**If you’re outraged, please chip in $5, or whatever you can, right now to build the grassroots power we need to defeat Republicans and make them pay for their cruelty.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250703-1?refcode=em20250703-2153&amount=5&refcode2=2153_21716_ZpMk9O&akid=2153%2E21716%2EZpMk9O)
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250703-1?amount=5&refcode=em20250703-2153&refcode2=2153_21716_ZpMk9O&akid=2153%2E21716%2EZpMk9O)**
— Sarah

name: David Hogg
subject: This is a crisis
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
We have to ask ourselves this question...
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20250703-1&t=1&akid=2339%2E587540%2ExbgcKk)
Hey Peter, it's David Hogg.
We have to ask ourselves: How do we build a Democratic Party that the working class wants to vote for?
Because right now, we're facing a _real_ problem.
Millions of people are leaving blue states and moving to the South, where their votes are gerrymandered and suppressed out of power.
We say we want to win back the working class. We say we want dramatic change.
But then we turn around and expect to do all that _without changing who’s in power_.
That's not going to work.
**The cast of characters that brought us here are not who’s going to get us out of here.**
If we’re serious about saving our democracy, if we’re serious about becoming the party of the people, not just the lesser evil — then we need to start acting like it.
That’s why Leaders We Deserve is building a healthy, people-powered culture of primaries.
**We believe that being in office for 30 years shouldn’t mean you get to coast through the next 10 without a challenge, especially not in the middle of a crisis.**
We are a democracy. We are the Democratic Party. We need to have a healthy culture of ensuring that people, including incumbents, can be challenged in primaries — and it’s not looked down on. It is embraced and celebrated.
We’re not doing this to be divisive. We’re doing this because our party has to dramatically change if it wants to survive and win elections again.
We need to tangibly show voters why we are not the least bad of two options, but why we are the best option. Period.
And that’s not going to happen with the same insiders spouting the same empty talking points year after year.
We need a new generation of leaders — with new ideas, new energy, and the courage to tell voters the truth and actually earn their trust. And yeah, that might make some folks in power uncomfortable.
But guess what? _That's the point_.
**Comfortable people — especially comfortable people with power — do not change. And right now, it is time for a change in management.**
This is a moment of crisis. We cannot keep tiptoeing around the truth.
Our democracy is being dismantled in broad daylight. And if we don’t meet this moment with urgency, courage, and new leadership — we will lose.
We have to change this culture and truly embrace democracy within our party if we are going to find our way out of this. Because no single person has the answer — but I can assure you, a healthy culture of competition is something our party can only stand to benefit from.
**At Leaders We Deserve, we’re not afraid to shake things up. We’re building the bench. We’re challenging the status quo. And we’re backing the kind of young, bold, progressive candidates who have the guts to do what this moment demands.**
But we need your support to do this work. We can only keep changing the game with folks like you stepping up to power our grassroots movement.
**[Peter, contribute $5 or anything you can right now to back us in this fight.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250703_whyprimariesmatter?refcode=em20250703-2339&amount=5&refcode2=2339_587540_xbgcKk&akid=2339%2E587540%2ExbgcKk)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250703_whyprimariesmatter?amount=5&refcode=em20250703-2339&refcode2=2339_587540_xbgcKk&akid=2339%2E587540%2ExbgcKk)**
With urgency and gratitude,
David Hogg
Leaders We Deserve
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20250703-1&t=2&refcode2=2339_587540_xbgcKk&akid=2339%2E587540%2ExbgcKk)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=2339%2E587540%2ExbgcKk)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=2339%2E587540%2ExbgcKk)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

name: Heidi Campbell
subject: The deed is done- what can we do?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Campbell for State Senate, Lee Jones, treasurer
body:
It's time to get off our screens, roll up our sleeves, and show up!| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110817614/555422851/129788657?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTUxNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmU3ZGY0Yy00ZTU4LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8I6Q1ld0t2beINnCQIYKjaHFCNqmEUUoyeeHIKXiJSc=&emci=ff0f8b36-4a58-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=2fe7df4c-4e58-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=43402173)
---
| | Peter, We all know that the bill passed by Congress a few minutes ago is an unconscionable and indefensible piece of legislation that will destroy our country. In tandem with Alligator Alcatraz and the daily deluge of inanity and pure evil emanating from the Magasphere, it’s clear that we (collectively) are the only ones who can save ourselves. It’s so painful to watch the America that we love (whether it ever really existed or not) absolutely demolished by a group united only by their selfishness, hatred, and greed. It’s unreal to witness the meteoric speed at which our norms and institutions are disintegrating under this sinister and depraved collection of reprobates who almost seem to be mocking the values, principles, and history of the American culture they claim to be defending in their revisionism. As sick and twisted as it all is, we’ve got to consider what we’ve done to contribute to this. Our complacency. Our lack of civic engagement. Our voracious consumption of cheap plastic crap. Our collective denial of responsibility to the environment and the global toll of our lifestyles. All of these things have helped bring us to this moment. And what exactly _is_ this moment? Please let this be ‘rock bottom.’ Because the only way we survive is by acknowledging that we have a problem — that we as individuals and as a community have drifted so far from what makes a society healthy, functional, and decent. Christian nationalists, billionaires, and fascist world leaders are determined to take away our rights, our freedom, and our votes. Here’s the truth: no amount of doom-scrolling, arguing in comment sections, or endlessly sharing political memes is going to fix this. We can’t tweet our way out of a broken country. We can’t comment our way out of broken communities. And we sure as hell can’t binge-watch our way into being better neighbors. We need to get up, get outside, and get to work. I’m regularly inspired by the good people in our community working through nonprofits, schools, and neighborhood groups to make the world a better place. It’s time for all of us to roll up our sleeves and put in the work to protect our environment for future generations. Over the past few months, my colleagues and I have been meeting with individuals, businesses, and nonprofits to build alternative funding and support for the life-saving work they do. We’ve found some solutions and secured some resources, but it’s going to take _all of us_ working together to find long-term answers. So please — **show up for your neighbors.** We’re living through a loneliness epidemic alongside all the political and ecological crises. You’d be shocked how far a simple gesture of human connection goes. Invite someone over. Offer to watch their kids. Mow a neighbor’s yard. Check in on the widow down the street. **Grab a bag, put on some gloves, and pick up litter.** It’s embarrassing how much trash we tolerate on our streets, in our parks, and along our streams. That’s our garbage. It’s also our responsibility. If we care about our communities looking like places worth loving, we have to act like it. **Reduce, reuse, and recycle.** We’re choking our planet under mountains of disposable packaging and single-use junk. Make small changes: compost your food scraps, refuse plastic bags, repair instead of replace, and support businesses trying to do it better. Join our [Tennessee Waste to Jobs Act](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110817615/555422852/-344041246?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTUxNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmU3ZGY0Yy00ZTU4LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8I6Q1ld0t2beINnCQIYKjaHFCNqmEUUoyeeHIKXiJSc=&emci=ff0f8b36-4a58-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=2fe7df4c-4e58-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=43402173) team (an amazing group of people)- we’re working to pass legislation that will bring ~$350M a year to our state in support for recycling infrastructure and education at no cost to taxpayers. We’ve spent so long staring into screens that we’ve forgotten what it means to belong to something real. We need to reclaim our country through real-world, face-to-face action. And maybe, just maybe, if enough of us decide that we’ve had enough of this despair and choose to act, we’ll find that the America we thought was slipping away still exists.
---

Tennessee](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110817616/555422853/-1019424588?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTUxNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmU3ZGY0Yy00ZTU4LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8I6Q1ld0t2beINnCQIYKjaHFCNqmEUUoyeeHIKXiJSc=&emci=ff0f8b36-4a58-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=2fe7df4c-4e58-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=43402173)** Nashville](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110817626/555422863/-1748843864?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTUxNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmU3ZGY0Yy00ZTU4LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8I6Q1ld0t2beINnCQIYKjaHFCNqmEUUoyeeHIKXiJSc=&emci=ff0f8b36-4a58-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=2fe7df4c-4e58-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=43402173)** — Connects thousands of volunteers with opportunities to serve nonprofits and schools citywide. clean_body: It's time to get off our screens, roll up our sleeves, and show up!| | | | | Peter, We all know that the bill passed by Congress a few minutes ago is an unconscionable and indefensible piece of legislation that will destroy our country. In tandem with Alligator Alcatraz and the daily deluge of inanity and pure evil emanating from the Magasphere, it’s clear that we (collectively) are the only ones who can save ourselves. It’s so painful to watch the America that we love (whether it ever really existed or not) absolutely demolished by a group united only by their selfishness, hatred, and greed. It’s unreal to witness the meteoric speed at which our norms and institutions are disintegrating under this sinister and depraved collection of reprobates who almost seem to be mocking the values, principles, and history of the American culture they claim to be defending in their revisionism. As sick and twisted as it all is, we’ve got to consider what we’ve done to contribute to this. Our complacency. Our lack of civic engagement. Our voracious consumption of cheap plastic crap. Our collective denial of responsibility to the environment and the global toll of our lifestyles. All of these things have helped bring us to this moment. And what exactly is this moment? Please let this be ‘rock bottom.’ Because the only way we survive is by acknowledging that we have a problem — that we as individuals and as a community have drifted so far from what makes a society healthy, functional, and decent. Christian nationalists, billionaires, and fascist world leaders are determined to take away our rights, our freedom, and our votes. Here’s the truth: no amount of doom-scrolling, arguing in comment sections, or endlessly sharing political memes is going to fix this. We can’t tweet our way out of a broken country. We can’t comment our way out of broken communities. And we sure as hell can’t binge-watch our way into being better neighbors. We need to get up, get outside, and get to work. I’m regularly inspired by the good people in our community working through nonprofits, schools, and neighborhood groups to make the world a better place. It’s time for all of us to roll up our sleeves and put in the work to protect our environment for future generations. Over the past few months, my colleagues and I have been meeting with individuals, businesses, and nonprofits to build alternative funding and support for the life-saving work they do. We’ve found some solutions and secured some resources, but it’s going to take all of us working together to find long-term answers. So please — show up for your neighbors. We’re living through a loneliness epidemic alongside all the political and ecological crises. You’d be shocked how far a simple gesture of human connection goes. Invite someone over. Offer to watch their kids. Mow a neighbor’s yard. Check in on the widow down the street. Grab a bag, put on some gloves, and pick up litter. It’s embarrassing how much trash we tolerate on our streets, in our parks, and along our streams. That’s our garbage. It’s also our responsibility. If we care about our communities looking like places worth loving, we have to act like it. Reduce, reuse, and recycle. We’re choking our planet under mountains of disposable packaging and single-use junk. Make small changes: compost your food scraps, refuse plastic bags, repair instead of replace, and support businesses trying to do it better. Join our team (an amazing group of people)- we’re working to pass legislation that will bring ~$350M a year to our state in support for recycling infrastructure and education at no cost to taxpayers. We’ve spent so long staring into screens that we’ve forgotten what it means to belong to something real. We need to reclaim our country through real-world, face-to-face action. And maybe, just maybe, if enough of us decide that we’ve had enough of this despair and choose to act, we’ll find that the America we thought was slipping away still exists. If you’re interested in volunteering or donating here are some groups I recommend: — Educates and mobilizes Tennesseans to end violence against women and girls. — Matches mentors with local kids to help them achieve their potential. — Provides books and literacy resources to underprivileged children across Nashville. — Supports Latino families with education, economic, and civic programs. — Works to protect democracy, voting rights, and progressive values in local politics. — Empowers artists impacted by homelessness to create and sell their work. — Helps formerly incarcerated men successfully re-enter the community. — Advances social and economic equity, fights voter suppression, and builds political power in communities of color. — Protects and preserves the Radnor Lake State Natural Area, a local environmental treasure. — Connects thousands of volunteers with opportunities to serve nonprofits and schools citywide. - promoting civic engagement for tweens- merging civics education with creative self-expression and community action. — Provides care and life skills for youth transitioning out of foster care. — Offers prevention, education, and support services for people affected by HIV/AIDS. — Advocates and raises funds to improve Nashville’s public schools. — (Often called The Nashville Good Project) — Grows, cooks, and shares nourishing food to build community and fight hunger. — A literary center hosting workshops, readings, and programs to build a vibrant writing community. — Provides reproductive health care, education, and advocacy. — Supports people returning from incarceration with employment, housing, and community reintegration. — Enhances the lives of adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities. — Collects and distributes food to those facing hunger across Middle Tennessee. — Provides healing services for survivors of sexual violence and education to prevent it. — Advocates for climate action, conservation, and environmental justice. — Offers sports training and competitions for individuals with intellectual disabilities. — Conserves farmland, forests, and natural resources that define Tennessee’s landscape. — A free grocery store in Nashville, founded by Brad and Kimberly Williams-Paisley, for individuals and families in crisis. — Advocates for the rights and independence of people with disabilities. — Promotes sustainable land use, clean water, and community tree planting. — Champions health care access and economic justice for vulnerable Tennesseans. — Builds a welcoming Tennessee by advocating for immigrants and refugees. — Supports innovative waste management policies that create jobs and protect the environment. — Helps women survivors of trafficking and addiction find healing, housing, and employment. -provides a nontraditional program for boys 12-18 years old that reside in the Tony Sudekum/J.C. Napier housing projects. — Works to eliminate racism, empower women, and end domestic violence. Heidi Campbell | | Senator Heidi Campbell is a Tennessee State Senator, representing District 20. subject: What kind of person votes to cut healthcare coverage?

Peter,

House Republicans just rammed through their so‐called “Big, Beautiful Bill” and let’s be clear: **This is one of the most dangerous attacks on working families we’ve seen yet.**

Here are the facts about what this disastrous budget will do:
* **Cut Medicaid for over 100,000 people across the district and over 600,000 folks across North Carolina** , many of whom rely on the program to access prescription medication, visits to the doctor and urgent care, and even life saving medical care.

* **Cut SNAP benefits for 375,000 North Carolinians and their families** \- leading to higher food insecurity across the state. An average family would be losing **$234/month** in food assistance.

* Cut Pell Grant funding and **increase student loan debt for over 97,000 North Carolina students** and families seeking a higher education.

* **Add $3 TRILLION to the national deficit** — while handing **$1 TRILLION dollars in tax breaks and subsidies** to the ultra-wealthy.

**Plain and simple, Peter: You can’t claim to support hardworking Americans and vote for this bill.** It’s cruel, it’s reckless, and it’s exactly what happens when Donald Trump and Republicans hold the gavel in both chambers.

If we want to stop this mess and protect American families, we have to flip the House in 2026. That work has already started, and we need your help to take back the majority.

[Will you pitch in $10 or whatever you can today to help my Democratic colleagues and I take back the House and put a stop to Republicans' extreme agenda that betrays the American people?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110817719/555423182/1371072993?amount=10&source=SBD_VF_EM_FR_070325_BBB_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhN2Y5YjQ5Ny00ZTU4LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=0oHG_8RYfnmypmy_nsmepMhttAiSrMVxNewZMp5_aaQ=) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now.

name: Jason Crow
subject: This work isn’t easy, but it’s important
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Congress [https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/logoJC%20(1).png]
Friend,

As we head into the Fourth of July weekend, I want to take a moment to reflect on the state of our nation and all the work we have left to do.

At a time when we are meant to celebrate our shared values of freedom, liberty, and opportunity for all of us, Republicans in Congress are working overtime to rip away Coloradans’ health care, sell off our public lands, jack up energy costs, and saddle future generations with trillions in debt all to serve the wealthiest few.

That’s not the America I believe in.

As an Army ranger, I stepped up to serve our nation. I served three combat tours in Iraq and Afghanistan — and learned a lot about servant leadership, working under pressure, and the value of selfless service to others.

From the halls of Congress, to the Armed Forces, to the streets of our communities, we are all tasked with renewing our commitment to defending our republic and everyone who lives here — no matter their background — and creating a country where we all have a fair shot at success.

Independence Day is not just a celebration of our nation’s birth, but a testament to the enduring strength of the shared values that bind us together.

The work isn’t easy, but it’s worth it. Together, we can build an America where our differences unite us, where hard work is rewarded, and where we can forge the path to a brighter tomorrow.

Can you make a donation now to help me keep fighting to build a nation for all of us in this critical moment? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-july2025]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

DONATE $10 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-july2025?amount=10&express_lane=true]
DONATE $25 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-july2025?amount=25&express_lane=true]

DONATE $50 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-july2025?amount=50&express_lane=true]
DONATE $100 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-july2025?amount=100&express_lane=true]

DONATE $250 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-july2025?amount=250&express_lane=true]
ANOTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-july2025]

Thank you for standing with me, and I hope you have a restful weekend.

— Jason
Jason Crow for Congress
8547 E Arapahoe Road, STE J-543
Greenwood Village, CO 80112
United States
www.jasoncrowforcongress.com [https://www.jasoncrowforcongress.com/] | info@jasoncrowforcongress.com [info@jasoncrowforcongress.com]

body:
Trump has signed an Executive Order targeting ActBlue - the tool millions of us use to fund the fight for abortion rights, LGBTQ+ equality, climate action, and more.
Chip in $7 right now to show we won’t be defunded or dismantled. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-abeo?refcode=070325_3]
FIRST: Trump launched a war on the free press - smearing journalists, silencing dissent, and undermining public trust in truth itself.
NEXT: He tried to overturn the election, fueled an insurrection, and installed loyalists across government to shield himself from accountability.
NOW: He’s targeting ActBlue with an Executive Order that instructs the DOJ to target and investigate it.
ActBlue is the infrastructure that powers everyything from school board races to the fight to flip the House. It’s the tool MILLIONS of grassroots donors use to fight for reproductive freedom, LGBTQ+ rights, climate action, and more.
And Trump has declared war on it.
We launched an emergency $25,000 response fund to push back. If we don’t act now, the right will see our silence as weakness. Chip in $7 or more before midnight to show we will not be silenced, defunded, or dismantled. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-abeo?refcode=070325_3]

GOP Rep. Steve Scalise **[fired back at Rep. Nancy Pelosi](https://substack.com/redirect/4bcf5574-0bb7-49ff-a9dd-b919a4cb3575?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after she delivered a scathing tirade against President Donald Trump's tax bill, calling her as well as other Democrats out for mischaracterizing the legislation and lying to the American people about what it does and does not include.
He did not hold back...
[Click To Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/4bcf5574-0bb7-49ff-a9dd-b919a4cb3575?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/4bcf5574-0bb7-49ff-a9dd-b919a4cb3575?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"I just want to remind all of you, tax cuts are expenditures!" Pelosi scolded Republicans. "They are the biggest spending in this bill, the tune of $5 trillion! Add itto the national debt to give tax cuts to the wealthiest people in this country,... You should be ashamed of yourselves!!"
Scalise could not let Pelosi go unchecked. "Why do we say there's going to be a Golden Era in this country? We're securing our border in this bill, we're opening up American energy in this bill, everywhere from the north slope of Alaska to the Gulf of America!" Scalise sounded off. "We were shut down!"
**["The billionaire doesn't care what the price of gasoline is,](https://substack.com/redirect/4bcf5574-0bb7-49ff-a9dd-b919a4cb3575?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** you know who does, the waitress making $32,000/year, she saves her tip money, maybe she can afford to go on that trip now because she can afford the gasoline!" he continued. "When America starts producing more energy, not only does it create more jobs, but all the bad guys around the world,... they're not going to have a cartel-like exclusive license on global energy anymore!"
**["Let's get this country back in the game!!"](https://substack.com/redirect/4bcf5574-0bb7-49ff-a9dd-b919a4cb3575?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** he Scalise erupted.
Watch the congressman put Pelosi in her place for complaining about America winning with the Big Beautiful Bill:
[Click To Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/4bcf5574-0bb7-49ff-a9dd-b919a4cb3575?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**Together with Goldco**
# **[The "Death Spiral" Threatening Your Savings](https://substack.com/redirect/e40471b5-39da-42a3-a735-105bcdde95ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# _Expect an economic "heart attack"..._
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e40471b5-39da-42a3-a735-105bcdde95ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Death spiral. **Economic heart attack**.
These are the **urgent warnings** from billionaire and founder of the world's largest hedge fund, Ray Dalio.
He's talking about the $36 Trillion national debt, and **only the ignorant** would dismiss his words...
Because it was **Dalio who tried warning the world** of a coming financial crisis in 2007.
The world **didn 't listen**...
But this time, **it 's not just Dalio ringing the alarm** bells...
**[Click Here to Keep Reading...](https://substack.com/redirect/e40471b5-39da-42a3-a735-105bcdde95ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ National Debt & Your Retirement](https://substack.com/redirect/e40471b5-39da-42a3-a735-105bcdde95ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# _**BREAKING:**_**House passes Trump 's 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act'**
**[The Republican-led House passed the sweeping One Big Beautiful Bill Act](https://substack.com/redirect/d8626d05-6c27-456d-a7fd-25c89e68a296?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in a narrow 218-214 vote earlier today, delivering a major legislative win for President Donald Trump ahead of his July 4 deadline.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d8626d05-6c27-456d-a7fd-25c89e68a296?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **The nearly 900-page bill extends Trump-era tax cuts,** increases defense and border security spending, and enacts deep cuts to Medicaid and discretionary spending.
* **The vote came after hours of tense negotiations and pressure,** including late-night social media posts from Trump, who warned Republicans that "MAGA IS NOT HAPPY" and demanded party unity. "There couldn't be a more engaged and involved president," said House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.).
* **Just two Republicans -- Reps. Thomas Massie (Ky.) and Brian Fitzpatrick (Pa.)** -- opposed the measure, while several GOP holdouts flipped under pressure. Delays included a record-breaking nearly nine-hour floor speech by House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) in protest of proposed Medicaid cuts.
* **The Senate passed a modified version of the bill earlier in the week,** increasing its projected deficit impact from $3 trillion to $3.9 trillion over a decade, according to the Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget. Despite internal GOP divides, leadership hinted at a follow-up executive action bills to address unresolved concerns over healthcare, tax deductions, and immigration.

* * *
# 21-year-old college intern gunned down in Washington D.C. tragedy
Eric Tarpinian-Jachym, **[a 21-year-old University of Massachusetts Amherst student and Congressional intern, died Monday following a triple shooting](https://substack.com/redirect/53640024-f520-4f1a-8969-220fdc3a75e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in Northwest Washington, D.C., police said.
* **Tarpinian-Jachym was interning for Rep. Ron Estes, R-Kan.** , and was found unconscious at the scene with gunshot wounds. Two others -- a woman and a 16-year-old boy -- were also shot but survived.
* **Authorities believe Tarpinian-Jachym was not the intended target** when multiple suspects opened fire on a group after exiting a vehicle at 7th and M Streets NW. "Please join Susan and me in praying for his family and respecting their privacy during this heartbreaking time," Rep. Estes said in a statement.
* **The UMass Amherst community mourned the loss of the rising senior** , who majored in finance with a political science minor. "We extend our deepest condolences to all who knew him," the university said in a statement. Tarpinian-Jachym was a Granby, Massachusetts, native and recent participant in the Washington Fellowship program.
* **A $25,000 reward is being offered for information leading to an arrest.** "His family is now without its son, and the University of Massachusetts Amherst will be missing a promising young graduate," said Massachusetts GOP Committeeman Chris Ryan. Anyone with information is asked to call 202-727-9099 or text the department's tip line at 50411.

* * *
# 'Hacktivist' steals decades of Columbia University student data in politically motivated attacked
A 'hacktivist' **[reportedly stole sensitive personal data](https://substack.com/redirect/dceeb035-d6e1-4ed5-adba-22ae2c4f09a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** from more than two million Columbia University students, applicants, and employees in a politically motivated cyberattack last week, officials confirmed.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/dceeb035-d6e1-4ed5-adba-22ae2c4f09a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **The June 24 breach temporarily knocked campus systems offline** and exposed Social Security numbers, citizenship status, admissions decisions, and employee salary records.
* **The attacker anonymously told Bloomberg News they targeted Columbia** to uncover documents related to affirmative action policies, banned by the U.S. Supreme Court in 2023. The hacker spent over two months infiltrating university servers before researching a 1.6-gigabyte load of information, which reportedly included decades worth of 2.5 million financial aid packages and applications.
* **University officials** said the breach did not affect Columbia's Irving Medical Center and that most systems have since been restored.
* **The incident comes as Columbia faces federal scrutiny** for its handling of antisemitism on campus and the loss of $400 million in federal contracts under the Trump administration. "Our team is working toward answers," school leadership said in an email to students, noting new cybersecurity safeguards are under review.

The incident comes as Columbia faces federal scrutiny for its handling of antisemitism on campus and the loss of $400 million in federal contracts under the Trump administration. "Our team is working toward answers," school leadership said in an email to students, noting new cybersecurity safeguards are under review. * PolitiBrawl is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support our work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Politibrawl 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

name: Autism Health Inc.
subject: Happy Independence Day from Autism Health Inc.
body:
|
---
| |
---
| | **Happy Independence Day from Autism Health Inc.**
---
| [ ](http://)
---
| Dear Supporters and Friends, As we celebrate the Fourth of July, we at **Autism Health Inc.** want to take a moment to reflect on the true meaning of this day... **freedom, courage, and sacrifice**. We are deeply grateful to the brave men and women who have served and those who continue to serve our country with honor and dedication. Their service makes it possible for all of us to live in a nation where we can raise our families, speak freely, and advocate for the change we believe in. Thank you for your sacrifice, your resilience, and your unwavering commitment to protecting the freedoms we hold dear. At Autism Health Inc., we are especially mindful of what **freedom** means for the families we serve. It is the freedom to choose the best path for your child. The freedom to access education, health, and resources without limitations. And the freedom to build a better future, one filled with **hope, healing, and empowerment**. Today and every day, we honor the spirit of independence and those who defend it. May this holiday bring you joy, peace, and a renewed sense of purpose in the work we do together. With heartfelt gratitude,
**The Autism Health Inc. Team**
[www.AutismHealth.com](https://autismhealth.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7c0b21e245e40197b3670bea2&id=2f1bda56f9&e=3a42edfdc4)

name: Voter Action Project Texas
subject: The vote suppressor's new gerrymander
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=]
Democracy Docket: Top GOP Map-Drawer and Right-Wing Law Firm Team Up to Target Texas Minority Voters [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=25.00&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=]
The fight against gerrymandering never ends, Team.
The opponents of voting rights who run one of Texas’s biggest counties have recruited the top conservative map-drawer, as well as a leading Washington, D.C.-based anti-voting group, to ram through a new gerrymander that aims to expand the U.S. House majority.
For background: Voters in Tarrant County — Texas’s third-largest county, with a population of around 2.2 million, and home to Fort Worth — elect four commissioners who, along with the County Judge Tim O’Hare, make up the county’s five-member governing body, known as commissioners court. (In Texas, a county judge is not a judicial officer, but a regular elected official.) O'Hare, for context, is an opponent of voting rights who tried to remove polling sites from college campuses ahead of the 2024 election.
Now that panel -- finalized in a 3-2 party-line vote -- has moved to redraw Tarrant County's maps and put local minority communities' right to fair representation at risk. The new map packs many of the county’s Black and brown voters into one heavily partisan district , switching the balance of the other district back in their direction. The map was shared publicly only in the past week and had not been discussed at public hearings.
Voter suppression anywhere changes the balance of Congress and hurts everyone, everywhere. Help us fight back for fair maps with $25 or whatever you can today and defend our fundamental right to vote >> [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=25.00&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=]
If you've saved your payment information with FastAction, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=15.00&tknfa=t99Y5rn_Y17kspY03jetYiRadYtFQbNS3U_m1bnLl5A=&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=25.00&tknfa=t99Y5rn_Y17kspY03jetYiRadYtFQbNS3U_m1bnLl5A=&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=50.00&tknfa=t99Y5rn_Y17kspY03jetYiRadYtFQbNS3U_m1bnLl5A=&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=100.00&tknfa=t99Y5rn_Y17kspY03jetYiRadYtFQbNS3U_m1bnLl5A=&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=250.00&tknfa=t99Y5rn_Y17kspY03jetYiRadYtFQbNS3U_m1bnLl5A=&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=20.00&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=]
This is all part of a sweeping national strategy that uses map-drawing to boost one party’s power at the expense of non-white voters and is now playing out at the local level.
How do we know? The D.C.-based attorney who oversaw much of these redistricting fights for a conservative law firm just took over as the top anti-voting lawyer at the MAGA version of the Justice Department.
The involvement from the start of national-level anti-voting operatives highlights the striking sophistication and coordination of this multi-pronged campaign to suppress democracy.
At a public hearing about the new map, Commissioner Matt Krause, a Republican, said, “ My entire intention is to allow Tarrant County to go from three Republicans, two Democrats on the commissioners court to four Republicans, one Democrat .” Within hours of the map vote, an anti-voting state lawmaker announced that he plans to run in the new district that’s been gerrymandered to favor his party.
Opponents of voting rights will stop at nothing in their pursuit of power and control. Please, chip in $25 or whatever you can to protect voting rights and fair representation >> [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=25.00&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=]
https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM= [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=]
Donate Now [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=C5c2p2Fjf/x3dOjmds48Kvon23jhj8Tz21x+xSpwsI2JDszavSBIr1gpYmf3Y8ocZxkltP0CWsFNguVnDV5+fkLz8u14/romSgfCq79ZVpM=]

## Trump’s ‘Big, Beautiful Bill’ gets House approval, next up is signing by president * WorldNetDaily * by Bob Unruh
House Speaker Mike Johnson confirmed that lawmakers are “delivering on our promise to make America great again.” Then the legislators voted 218-214 to adopt Senate changes to President Donald Trump’s “Big, Beautiful Bill.” It’s a landmark for tax cuts and spending that includes many of Trump’s priorities. | | **_Meet Russell:_** Born and raised in Surfside Beach, SC, Congressman Russell Fry is the proud and homegrown Representative for South Carolina’s 7th Congressional District. Growing up in a cinder block home with no heating or air conditioning, Russell learned at an early age that hard work and perseverance pave the only true path to the American Dream. He watched his parents work day in and day out to make ends meet. And the values they instilled in him during his upbringing led Russell to self-fund both his undergraduate education at the University of South Carolina, and later his law studies at the Charleston School of Law. At Charleston, Russell served as the President of the Student Bar Association and even helped the school achieve its American Bar Association accreditation. He went on to practice law in the Grand Strand area for over a decade before entering the political arena to serve in the South Carolina House of Representatives for State House District 106 (Horry County). During his seven-year tenure in the SC House, Russell fought to safeguard Constitutional liberties, lower taxes, and put the best interests of South Carolina families above all else. He climbed the ranks to become Chief Majority Whip, and also chaired the House Opioid Prevention Study Committee. Under Russell’s leadership, 18 policy initiatives to combat the opioid crisis were signed into law. Since the first day he stepped foot in Washington, Russell has strived to shake up the corrupt status quo and deliver real change for the American people. He believes adamantly that so long as the American people stand united, we can keep the American Dream alive for every citizen of our great nation. Russell is a dedicated husband to his wife, Bronwen, and a loving father to his son, James. The family lives in Murrells Inlet with their chocolate lab, Jasper.

clean_body: Help lay the groundwork for the president’s third-quarter agenda. | | | | | | We'll get right to the point, Peter. | | President Trump’s administration is lacking key poll data in | | | | | | | | | As one of President Trump’s most dependable allies in Congress, Russell Fry was tasked with garnering essential feedback from patriots like YOU. And he’s got every member of his opinion research team reaching out to help DJT . We pulled your file earlier this morning, and you’re the EXACT type of Republican we need to hear from at this critical juncture of Q3. Peter, with Democrats working tirelessly to IMPEACH President Trump for a THIRD TIME , we have no time to waste in gauging the public’s opinion on Agenda 47. | | | | | Thank you for your time, Team Fry | | | | | | | | | Meet Russell: Born and raised in Surfside Beach, SC, Congressman Russell Fry is the proud and homegrown Representative for South Carolina’s 7th Congressional District. Growing up in a cinder block home with no heating or air conditioning, Russell learned at an early age that hard work and perseverance pave the only true path to the American Dream. He watched his parents work day in and day out to make ends meet. And the values they instilled in him during his upbringing led Russell to self-fund both his undergraduate education at the University of South Carolina, and later his law studies at the Charleston School of Law. At Charleston, Russell served as the President of the Student Bar Association and even helped the school achieve its American Bar Association accreditation. He went on to practice law in the Grand Strand area for over a decade before entering the political arena to serve in the South Carolina House of Representatives for State House District 106 (Horry County). During his seven-year tenure in the SC House, Russell fought to safeguard Constitutional liberties, lower taxes, and put the best interests of South Carolina families above all else. He climbed the ranks to become Chief Majority Whip, and also chaired the House Opioid Prevention Study Committee. Under Russell’s leadership, 18 policy initiatives to combat the opioid crisis were signed into law. Since the first day he stepped foot in Washington, Russell has strived to shake up the corrupt status quo and deliver real change for the American people. He believes adamantly that so long as the American people stand united, we can keep the American Dream alive for every citizen of our great nation. Russell is a dedicated husband to his wife, Bronwen, and a loving father to his son, James. The family lives in Murrells Inlet with their chocolate lab, Jasper. | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | Team Fry is incredibly grateful for your generosity and support. If you’re not in a position to donate, we would NEVER want you to do so. You can still make an impact by signing up to volunteer below! | | | | | We have new merchandise lines set to release throughout the course of 2025. Click the link below to visit Team Fry’s official online store and grab your gear today! | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ⬇DONATE BY CHECK⬇_ Russell Fry for Congress PO Box 14641 Surfside Beach, SC 29587 | | | | | | | | | | | | |

name: VoteVets.org
subject: We are going to hammer them over this
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
To all our Republican friends in swing districts: Expect to hear from us
#
These are the moments VoteVets was made for.
The Republican Party has complete control of government. Instead of using that power to protect Veterans and the services they depend on, they have directly targeted them.
The budget the GOP just passed and that Trump is about to sign is an attack on Veterans. It is an attack on Military Families. It is an attack on people working paycheck to paycheck, struggling, just trying to get by.
And we are not going to let people forget that. To all our Republican friends in swing states and House districts: **Expect to hear from us.**
[**VoteVets will hold Trump and the GOP accountable. Rush a donation to the Patriot Fund now and help us hammer them relentlessly through next year’s elections.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-july25.sScHXv&refcode=em250703-1-120&t=1&akid=34468%2E3013755%2EhYRRyZ)

| | Peter, I promised the good people of NC-13 that I would fight to secure the border, lower taxes, reduce government waste, and increase penalties for illegal immigrants. _And I am excited to say we took giant steps forward on those promises._**I’m fighting each and every day to ensure every American will be safer and working, middle-class Americans will keep more of their paychecks.** ✅ Cutting taxes for the middle-class & seniors ✅ NO taxes on tips & NO taxes on overtime ✅ Over $3 trillion in wasteful spending cuts✅ Increasing funding for ICE, Border Patrol, and the Coast Guard to stop illegal immigration and the flow of fentanyl ✅ Building the wall Thank you for your trust and support. God Bless,
---

As the only Democrat in the country who can flip a Governor's seat blue in 2025, I feel the pressure to _win big_ at the ballot box. Experts are calling this race a toss-up and our opponents are launching new attacks every day, so I'm asking my strongest supporters for a small contribution.

name: Sara Jacobs
subject: A damn shame
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em]
House Republicans have passed their cruel, immoral, disastrous budget.
House Republicans have voted to kick millions of people off their health insurance.
House Republicans have voted to make millions more Americans go hungry.
They wanted to pass this bill in the middle of the night — sneaking around and hiding from the public because they *know* how unpopular and devastating it is. We didn’t let them do that, and I’m glad they were forced to put their names on this legislation in broad daylight for everyone to see.
So how did we get here? And to answer a question I know some may be wondering, why didn’t Democrats stop this from passing?
The reality is that we tried really, really hard. We pushed behind the scenes, we negotiated, we did everything we could to try to make our Republican colleagues understand the real human consequences of what they’re doing.
Despite hearing all of that, despite hearing from their constituents, House Republicans have the majority, and they voted to pass this horrible bill.
Why? Because they’re more scared of Donald Trump than they are of their constituents and voters.
Because they want to give their campaign donors, billionaires, and corporations a tax break.
And because it turns out you can’t force someone to care about other people.
Look, I took the red eye to D.C. two nights ago. Last night I slept on my couch in my office. And I would go 10, 20, 30 more nights without sleeping to protect the American people — that’s what our job is. And it’s a damn shame that my Republican colleagues can’t see that.
I’ll leave you with this final thought: It is abundantly clear that if we have any hope of passing legislation that will help people in this country and blocking more terrible legislation, we absolutely must win back a Democratic majority in the House next year.
I am in this fight to win, and I will never stop working to improve my constituents’ lives and to elect more strong Democratic leaders who will do the same.
I’m glad to have you by my side in this. I hope you’re able to enjoy some time with loved ones this holiday weekend.
Sincerely,
Sara
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em]
Congresswoman Sara Jacobs
California’s 51st District

name: Fight Like Hell PAC
subject: The GOP is showing us what happens when the wrong people hold power.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight Like Hell PAC
body:
We’re building a stronger bench of future leaders |
#
---
Team, There’s real momentum behind campaigns that put working families first. Just last month, we saw what can be achieved when a movement stays relentlessly focused on people over politics. At the same time, Republicans in Congress just passed the “One Big Beautiful Bill” — legislation that rips away health care and food assistance from millions of Americans by cutting Medicaid and SNAP so that they can give the rich a tax cut. **Recent wins on the campaign trail prove what’s possible, while the GOP is showing us exactly what’s at stake when the wrong people hold power.** We need more leaders willing to fight for working families, protect our freedoms, and deliver real results. That’s why Governor Whitmer is so focused on recruiting and supporting the next generation of candidates — people ready to stand up, speak out, and make change in their communities. **[Look around. You might be the person we’ve all been waiting for. If you’ve ever thought about running for office, there’s never been a more important time to take the leap. The House just passed Trump’s Big Ugly Scam of a bill. |

Peter, **The House — including all 10 North Carolina U.S. House Republicans — just passed Trump’s Big Ugly Scam of a bill.** Republicans in Congress have caved to Trump’s pressure, betraying their promises to the American people. This handout to billionaires balloons the deficit and puts health care at risk for millions. _Here’s just how bad it gets:_ \- Cuts Medicaid, threatening rural hospitals and nursing homes, and ripping health care coverage away from 600,000 North Carolinians
\- Slashes funding for vital programs that feed hungry kids
\- Raises energy costs by a staggering 13% right here in North Carolina
\- Adds $4 trillion to the federal deficit so billionaires can enrich themselves further This is a betrayal, plain and simple. Since launching my U.S. Senate campaign, I've been on the ground, holding town halls and listening to North Carolinians who are struggling with rising costs and budget cuts —**this is going to make lives so much harder.** That’s why I led demonstrators outside Thom Tillis’s Senate office, demanding he oppose this harmful legislation. Our public pressure worked — Tillis opposed the bill, leading to Trump forcing his retirement. Now that it just cleared the House, **we must respond swiftly and with strength.** [**Will you join me in holding Washington Republicans accountable for their betrayal to the American people and bringing principled leadership to Washington? name: Jim Schultz
subject: This Independence Day, Let’s Say It Plainly: America Is a Great Country
body:
As we celebrate this Independence Day, it is worth saying something too often left unsaid by some of our political and cultural leaders: America is...

| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/bfa60cbb12fc/the-briefing-from-mpbc-13056136?e=baf210d431)
---
| | | | |
---
| Friend,
As we celebrate this Independence Day, it is worth saying something too often left unsaid by some of our political and cultural leaders: America is a great country.
There is now a view among our elites and some leaders that patriotism is a little embarrassing. Sometimes the sentiment is more hostile, rooted in the belief that America is a morally compromised nation and that there is little or nothing for which our nation should be proud. A Democrat member of Congress from Minnesota recently called the United States “one of the worst countries in the world.” She is profoundly wrong.
Our nation, like all nations, is imperfect. No nation built by human hands can escape the fallenness of human nature. But we are a great nation with a proud and worthy history, and we should not let our home’s imperfection blind us to its greatness.
This country—our country—has done more good, for more people, than any other in history. We have fought and won just wars, asking for nothing in return but the land on which to bury our dead. Our system of government has through time durably protected the rights of its citizens and cultivated their well-being like no other. The generosity of our people has lifted out of poverty and destitution millions at home and abroad. And we are founded upon, and measure ourselves against, what is among the noblest of beliefs: that all men are created equal, and that each person is endowed by the Creator with inalienable rights.
This Independence Day, we ought to be clear-eyed about our challenges, yes—but also confident. We must not surrender to the fashionable cynicism of our time. We should instead meet the moment with the pride, resolve, and gratitude that this country still deserves.
America is a great nation. Our leaders should say that more often. And this Independence Day, we all should call it to mind. We should wave our flag proudly.
Happy 4th of July to you and your family, and may God bless you and our great nation.
Working with you,
**Jim Schultz** PresidentMinnesota Private Business Council
---
|
---
| **About the Minnesota Private Business Council:** The Minnesota Private Business Council advocates for commonsense policies supporting Minnesota industry and innovation and benefiting Minnesota’s workers, families, and communities. Comprised of privately held businesses from a broad array of Minnesota industries, the Minnesota Private Business Council advocates for policies leading to job growth, new business creation and entrepreneurship, and broad-based well-being for Minnesota’s workers and their families. clean_body: As we celebrate this Independence Day, it is worth saying something too often left unsaid by some of our political and cultural leaders: America is... | | | | | | | | | | | | Friend, As we celebrate this Independence Day, it is worth saying something too often left unsaid by some of our political and cultural leaders: America is a great country. There is now a view among our elites and some leaders that patriotism is a little embarrassing. Sometimes the sentiment is more hostile, rooted in the belief that America is a morally compromised nation and that there is little or nothing for which our nation should be proud. A Democrat member of Congress from Minnesota recently called the United States “one of the worst countries in the world.” She is profoundly wrong. Our nation, like all nations, is imperfect. No nation built by human hands can escape the fallenness of human nature. But we are a great nation with a proud and worthy history, and we should not let our home’s imperfection blind us to its greatness. This country—our country—has done more good, for more people, than any other in history. We have fought and won just wars, asking for nothing in return but the land on which to bury our dead. Our system of government has through time durably protected the rights of its citizens and cultivated their well-being like no other. The generosity of our people has lifted out of poverty and destitution millions at home and abroad. And we are founded upon, and measure ourselves against, what is among the noblest of beliefs: that all men are created equal, and that each person is endowed by the Creator with inalienable rights. This Independence Day, we ought to be clear-eyed about our challenges, yes—but also confident. We must not surrender to the fashionable cynicism of our time. We should instead meet the moment with the pride, resolve, and gratitude that this country still deserves. America is a great nation. Our leaders should say that more often. And this Independence Day, we all should call it to mind. We should wave our flag proudly. Happy 4th of July to you and your family, and may God bless you and our great nation. Working with you, Jim Schultz PresidentMinnesota Private Business Council | | About the Minnesota Private Business Council: The Minnesota Private Business Council advocates for commonsense policies supporting Minnesota industry and innovation and benefiting Minnesota’s workers, families, and communities. Comprised of privately held businesses from a broad array of Minnesota industries, the Minnesota Private Business Council advocates for policies leading to job growth, new business creation and entrepreneurship, and broad-based well-being for Minnesota’s workers and their families. For more information, please visit . | Follow the Minnesota Private Business Council: | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright (C) 2025 Minnesota Private Business Council. All rights reserved.

50th Speaker of the United States House of Representatives, Newt Gingrich joins me today. Newt's new book Trump's Triumph is a New York Times best seller. Newt documents President Trump's greatest comeback in political history. Despite a 9 year effort to destroy him, President Trump won popular and electoral votes and became the first president to be nonconsecutively re-elected since President Grover Cleveland. Now that the big, beautiful bill has passed the Senate, President Trump is one step closer to fulfilling his agenda and the promise he made on the campaign trail. If President Trump can keep the House in 2026, the MAGA revolution will be fully institutionalized and set the base for Republicans to win the general election in 2028.

> **Blue Amp just launched The Soundcheck Fund (TSF) to elevate independent media voices --**_**by donating five figures out of our own pockets. We ask that you help us grow the fund[ by simply getting a paid subscription to Blue Amp](https://substack.com/redirect/b86883fd-7108-47e4-ba18-c5ac15716d99?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for .26 per day.**_
> **We 've waited for liberal billionaires. ENOUGH. We'll build an ecosystem of bold, truth-tellers--commentators, journalists, etc--with grants and promotion.**
> **There 's a link to the app on our main page. Tired of being gaslit, silenced, misinformed? **_**[Get A Paid Subscription](https://substack.com/redirect/b86883fd-7108-47e4-ba18-c5ac15716d99?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_**, join a movement** _**that won 't be stopped**_**.**
#### **🔵 Subscribe now! Power indy media & **_**our**_**democratic future**
* * *
### Today Was An Important One To Declare Our Corporate-Media Independence
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3d16448a-d200-41c9-9e91-453ab18c2a5d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In this energizing episode, Cliff and David sit down with Tim Fullerton--a veteran of the Obama administration and the rising progressive podcast _The Find Out_ --to unpack how storytelling, independent media, and smart digital strategy can reshape the political landscape ahead of 2026.
Tim shares the unlikely origin of his podcast, which grew from a grassroots campaign ("White Dudes for Harris") into a viral show that now boasts nearly half a million downloads in just four months. The podcast, co-hosted by five politically active men across generations--_including a literal Nazi hunter_ --aims to speak directly to male voters in a way the left rarely does.
The trio then shifted to urgent political terrain: the devastating "One Big Beautiful Bill "(OBBB), AKA The Ugly, Stupid, Corrupt, MAGA Monstrosity (ATUSCMM) just passed by the Trump-indentured Congress. Tim calls it "The Big Bullshit Bill"--a cruel wealth transfer that rips away health care from 17 million Americans while handing billionaires another round of tax cuts.
_**Cliff sharpens the messaging: "They want people to die so Elon Musk can buy yacht slippers.** " Or so weirdo James Bond villain, Peter Thiel, can acquire more cyborg parts to live forever (this is actually true, it's what he's trying to do, believe it or not)._
The conversation drills into why the Democratic Party's failed to build emotional resonance around policies like SNAP, Medicaid, and rural hospitals--and what independent media voices can do to fix that.
Tim emphasizes the power of breaking down numbers into real human stories. Like a friend of his, from his home state of Maine, with stage 4 cancer. _He now may lose his Medicaid, and with it, his life._
Together, they call for a robust, decentralized digital army--"a thousand Joe Rogans of the left"--to talk to every demographic in their own language, with their own trusted messengers. Cliff discusses his **Soundcheck Fund** , _which now includes a personal five-figure investment in independent creators to create an ecosystem_.
Tim urges listeners to follow, support, and amplify each other--because the Democratic Party sure won't do it. It actually brings to mind the old Will Rogers line, "I am not a member of any organized party--I am a Democrat."
Meanwhile, corporate media is out to lunch--of the three martini variety.
The show wraps with a sober warning: Trump and the GOP are already laying the groundwork to cheat in 2026. It will take millions of small actions--by these very same independent creators Cliff's Blue Amp is trying to fund, as well as from every "influencer" with a smartphone--to hold the line on democracy. It is a challenge we must embrace--we're not Trump with those bone spurs, after all.
We must take this on--and WIN.
`Full Blue Amp Subscription For Just .26/Day!`
_**Get The Truth corporate media ignores from a former Biden ad man & former Emmy-winning MSNBC anchor who know and will tell What Is Really Going On.**_
_**And support our new *Soundcheck Fund* to help fund & promote independent media!**_
_**Amplify Good. Support Indy Media!**_
